| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

clean_body: Some Prime members are eligible for a welcome bonus worth hundreds, issued after approval.| | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | Hello, There’s an update related to your Amazon Prime account. subject: Your file looks WRONG, Peter
body:

subject: Federal Investigators Scrutinize Deqa Dhalac’s Past Nonprofit Ties Amid Expanding Medicaid Fraud Probe
body:
Here's the latest breaking news at The Maine Wire

** Federal Investigators Scrutinize Deqa Dhalac’s Past Nonprofit Ties Amid Expanding Medicaid Fraud Probe (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=666ab6f5a3&e=f093fa355c)
------------------------------------------------------------
By Jon Fetherston on Dec 26, 2025 06:56 am
A widening investigation by the U.S. House Oversight and Accountability Committee into alleged Medicaid fraud has placed new scrutiny on the background and political career of Deqa Dhalac, a Democratic lawmaker from South Portland whose prior leadership role at a Maine nonprofit has become a focal point of state and federal review and an issue in [...]

------------------------------------------------------------

After its Christmas break concludes, Congress is set to return in January with a host of unfinished business that could shape the political terrain of 2026. A short-term funding bill that ended the historic government shutdown in November expires on Jan. 30. Legislators have yet to implement healthcare reform following a new public focus on affordability in a GOP-controlled [...]

A sweeping crime crackdown launched by the Trump administration, including the controversial deployment of National Guard troops in the nation’s capital, has coincided with a steep nationwide decline in murders, according to newly released crime data, even as the effort faces mounting legal and political challenges. A new statewide economic analysis released this month paints a stark picture of prosperity split sharply between the greater Portland metropolitan area and much of rural Maine, with data showing decades of uneven growth, persistent joblessness and widening disparities in job quality and basic economic security. The State of Working Maine 2025 report from the [...]

Former prosecutor and well-known TV crime fighter Nancy Grace is spotlighting a suspiciously-missing Maine girl. On her Christmas-eve show, “Crime Stories on Fox Nation,” Grace featured the disappearance of Stefanie Damron of New Sweden, a tiny Aroostook County town, population 577. Damron, 13, allegedly walked into the woods near her family’s off-the-grid wood-framed yurt in [...]

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. | | | THIS RFK Jr shocker is so outrageous... it could get him impeached: . Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. Just announced: Every dollar you give is **MATCHED** from now until midnight on 12/31, up to $50,000. Peter,
For many Americans, 2025 was the year that redistricting turned from a wonky political process to a galvanizing issue and driving force for a movement of Americans rising up and taking back our democracy. We watched as cynical politicians sought to manipulate maps in states across the country — all because they’re scared of losing power.
To harness this moment, we reimagined our grassroots movement and unveiled its new name: **Redistricting Action** — together, we are building the powerhouse organization we need for the existential fights ahead. Voters need tools to defend their representation right now. That is what Redistricting Action is built to provide – a modern platform for grassroots activists who want clear information, better tools, and direct ways to engage in the redistricting process when it matters most.
Because this is no longer about an abstract process._It’s an ongoing struggle for representative democracy that demands vigilance, sustained organizing, and grassroots activists to stay engaged in the fight. _
As we look to 2026, we more than have our work cut out for us. In the very first days of January, as many as eight states could begin a mid-decade redraw, either to expand the extreme nationwide power grab or to mobilize to counter those efforts. That list includes: Kansas, Nebraska, Florida, New Hampshire, South Carolina, Virginia, Illinois, and Maryland.
**Redistricting Action needs to give these fights, wherever and whenever they arise, all we’ve got. But that takes enormous resources, powered by grassroots supporters like you, Peter.**
[**That’s why we’re announcing a $50,000 MATCH to raise the critical funds we need to meet this moment. Don’t wait — this offer expires on December 31. Give today when your impact can be DOUBLED in the fight to preserve our representative democracy.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121981991/591225890/798345718?refcode=don_aotl_tier1_email_StateofPlay_email_20251226-AOTL-Tier1-StateofPlay_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251226-AOTL-Tier1-StateofPlay_&utm_campaign=StateofPlay&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjZiYjBkM2QxLTVhZTItZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=O7l8yXTp0PXzemIe3SXEleMuDKI8AP70XHVve7Ukg14=)

clean_body: You can help us meet these challenges tomorrow with a MATCHED donation today. | Today, we're in the midst of a redistricting crisis — and our democracy needs to be actively defended every day. That's why All On The Line is nowRedistricting Action — a grassroots movement built for the critical fights ahead. Thank you for being part of it. Just announced: Every dollar you give is MATCHED from now until midnight on 12/31, up to $50,000. Give now and help Redistricting Action prepare for the toughest fights ahead of us >> | = Peter, For many Americans, 2025 was the year that redistricting turned from a wonky political process to a galvanizing issue and driving force for a movement of Americans rising up and taking back our democracy. We watched as cynical politicians sought to manipulate maps in states across the country — all because they’re scared of losing power. To harness this moment, we reimagined our grassroots movement and unveiled its new name: Redistricting Action — together, we are building the powerhouse organization we need for the existential fights ahead. Voters need tools to defend their representation right now. That is what Redistricting Action is built to provide – a modern platform for grassroots activists who want clear information, better tools, and direct ways to engage in the redistricting process when it matters most. Because this is no longer about an abstract process.It’s an ongoing struggle for representative democracy that demands vigilance, sustained organizing, and grassroots activists to stay engaged in the fight. As we look to 2026, we more than have our work cut out for us. In the very first days of January, as many as eight states could begin a mid-decade redraw, either to expand the extreme nationwide power grab or to mobilize to counter those efforts. That list includes: Kansas, Nebraska, Florida, New Hampshire, South Carolina, Virginia, Illinois, and Maryland. Redistricting Action needs to give these fights, wherever and whenever they arise, all we’ve got. But that takes enormous resources, powered by grassroots supporters like you, Peter. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Your support is deeply appreciated by every member of the Redistricting Action team, and the thousands of volunteers in states across the country. # **▶️: Healthcare is a fundamental, human right.**
I personally know we need a better system because I, too, am underinsured.
| [](https://1xz3r.mjt.lu/lnk/AWUAAImvTDIAAc6uGOwAA-Neh6MAAYKKXNoAoUWGADIMFwBpToiFLkrPL0lBQDmZdiGxG9fmVQAtq28/2/QIgI83PZe6cwoZ6gf48x5A/aHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYWN0Ymx1ZS5jb20vZG9uYXRlL2hlYWx0aGNhcmUtaXMtYS1odW1hbi1yaWdodA)
---
Quality healthcare is not a privilege reserved for those of a particular financial status but rather a fundamental human right.It’s time our representatives in Congress recognize that basic fact and take steps to fix our healthcare system.**_It will take grassroots campaigns like mine to shift the dynamic in Washington. That’s why I’m asking for your support right now to build the momentum we need to flip Texas 8 and achieve victory for EVERY uninsured and underinsured person across our country._**

subject: Trump’s DOJ pressure campaign
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Democratic Association of Secretaries of State, Treasurer Denise Merrill
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121986318/591228592/-664592562?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMzM5ODc2YS02MGUyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RMlqYN4tEktOuT0l2mESkc8EDrUAyBqglC-t-tpVKYc=)
**➤➤ BREAKING: Trump’s DOJ offers states confidential deal to remove voters flagged by feds**
* * *
Friend, this is disturbing. Trump’s DOJ is quietly pressuring states to sign confidential deals giving the federal government power to flag and remove voters from the rolls – all behind closed doors. **At least 13 states have received these draft agreements – and in 11 of them, Republican officials have signaled they’re ready to comply.** Under these secret agreements, states would be required to hand over unredacted voter data – including Social Security numbers and driver’s licenses – and then purge anyone the DOJ flags as “ineligible” within 45 days. Let’s be clear: states control elections – not the federal government, and certainly not Trump’s DOJ. Two Democratic-led states – Colorado and Wisconsin – have already said no. But if Trump gets his way, we’re looking at a blueprint for mass voter purges, a national voter surveillance system, and unprecedented federal overreach. **[Can you rush in $3 or whatever you can today to help us fight back against this dangerous federal overreach?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121986320/591228593/-248329304?refcode=em-12.26.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMzM5ODc2YS02MGUyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RMlqYN4tEktOuT0l2mESkc8EDrUAyBqglC-t-tpVKYc=)** __If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: __ | | | **[Chip in $10 Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121986322/591228594/-248329304?refcode=em-12.26.25-all-a&express_lane=true&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMzM5ODc2YS02MGUyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RMlqYN4tEktOuT0l2mESkc8EDrUAyBqglC-t-tpVKYc=)**

body:
When something is broken, you don't complain — you fix it. |
---

[](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=5173b3553fd048ddb3399beb2d82eb6b&_e=YHo8H7tiSQbt9xuzkSY8niF6LyXoYgbRsFXftt-wUBhXic1Xwy5NmkKS1T-CumhaLORg7l011gT9bFsvADtUGzW2jQR3ILLgn6w5Lqxtvjly3BArjHRj0EQaX2H9l5SrN2sodnHD6mzsy0iSTIaknSMbGZUu4xrujym5DNnr3yZO02KEZeDJu0Veogj226y4lAEkaYkHeIT-xfkCUQSBT9N4dFmIESQ1r0G-Y8fCufkvAeyMe_Amr1B0632piM2I9wptIegAHKN6EzPmL-tU8_52_ddIugc6mGZShncc4zCQ2dNnoB9xgU8aRYLG5f1nvu5zcslt9oTqod1tOL1VSvJTOME-nyCLS_v7Y-40Tqc5J6totfc1WP_WCJXqTAsCrJnxAG4JkpgKQajrzmPVVX2Ekd5SZKO2y4OkNGp4mUNyr-ZEmtOPkF-35NYlUVfJxw1VGY5iqasHbViF_ZSz7JZUWMRmyFporIFMS9EXZ9pFjxHuCItH_VH2ERWLMCckj8g-lQAfE3p47NGPqBFYCCXpLAGpxcAody-S5YQWQ7zkQF-g_c1f94dDTSGpm83aJPxUgIUjVE0PUeFaKQsoMzwiGhF_dMswh2vujcqiokVTE7mc9rgFj_mTpsM_WDIIbkSFQbctRcnxfG1fngZdOdF0vgXwYU_OpkACcDFZ2sw%3D)

Fellow Patriot,

Long before I ever put on a badge, I made a simple promise to myself:

When something is broken, you don't complain - _you fix it._

That philosophy guided me through eight years as Sheriff. It's why I stood up to cartels, why I defended our border communities, and why I backed our deputies even when it wasn't politically convenient.

But today, something bigger is broken.

Washington has stopped listening to the people who make this country work. Not the lobbyists. Not the bureaucrats. _The people_.

The ranchers who wake up before dawn. The small business owners who sign both sides of the paycheck. The parents who are fighting to protect their kids' future. The families who still believe in God, Family, and Freedom.

They deserve a voice that won't fold when things get tough.

**That's why I'm running for Congress - not to join the political class, but to challenge it.** To restore the kind of leadership that answers to the people, not to the unelected and unaccountable. And I'm going to fight for that every single day. But I can't do it alone.

clean_body: When something is broken, you don't complain — you fix it. | Fellow Patriot, Long before I ever put on a badge, I made a simple promise to myself: When something is broken, you don't complain - you fix it. That philosophy guided me through eight years as Sheriff. It's why I stood up to cartels, why I defended our border communities, and why I backed our deputies even when it wasn't politically convenient. But today, something bigger is broken. Washington has stopped listening to the people who make this country work. Not the lobbyists. Not the bureaucrats. The people. The ranchers who wake up before dawn. The small business owners who sign both sides of the paycheck. The parents who are fighting to protect their kids' future. The families who still believe in God, Family, and Freedom. They deserve a voice that won't fold when things get tough. That's why I'm running for Congress - not to join the political class, but to challenge it. To restore the kind of leadership that answers to the people, not to the unelected and unaccountable. And I'm going to fight for that every single day. But I can't do it alone. If you believe America needs leaders who show up, tell the truth, and stand firm - Thank you for being in this fight, Mark Lamb Republican for Congress, AZ-5 Join Mark Lamb on social media! | | PAID FOR BY MARK LAMB FOR CONGRESS Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go: .

name: Haley Stevens
subject: This picture says it all
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
From a little girl in Michigan ...

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1735%2E49239%2EeQdC8w)
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10830?t=2&akid=1735%2E49239%2EeQdC8w)
---
[](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10831?t=3&akid=1735%2E49239%2EeQdC8w)
That’s me! A little girl growing up in Michigan who just wanted to help people.

She never imagined she’d serve in a president’s administration, be elected to Congress, or run for the U.S. Senate to represent her beloved home state.
She never imagined she’d get to spend her life fighting for the Michigan families she loves so much.

But that’s exactly what I’m doing. Because everywhere I go, I hear the same thing: Michiganders are scared.
Scared of rising costs. Scared of losing their health care. Scared about what Trump will do next.

null, I love this state. And I refuse to sit back as the MAGA chaos agenda harms the people I love.

I’m stepping up – and I’m asking you to do the same.
**[Will you chip in $5 to help me win this battleground race and keep Michigan blue?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10832?t=4&akid=1735%2E49239%2EeQdC8w)**
| | [Donate](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10831?t=5&akid=1735%2E49239%2EeQdC8w)
---
Fighting for you,

Haley
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10833?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=6&akid=1735%2E49239%2EeQdC8w) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10834?t=7&akid=1735%2E49239%2EeQdC8w)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley

name: Jon Ossoff
subject: New York Times: "People are going silent"
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
body:
#

Peter,
Before I ran for the U.S. Senate, I was an investigative journalist leading a team that exposed corruption and human rights abuses worldwide.
This report in _The New York Times_ reminded me of what I saw in many of those investigations:
[](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.lXrKfY&refcode=em251226-1-f&t=1&akid=1323%2E5691347%2ECShYmf)
More and more, the atmosphere under Trump reminds me of countries where dissent is silenced by intimidation.
From the article:
_Fired federal workers who are worried about losing their homes ask not to be quoted by name. University presidents fearing that millions of dollars in federal funding could disappear are holding their fire. Chief executives alarmed by tariffs that could hurt their businesses are on mute. ..._
_“When you see important societal actors — be it university presidents, media outlets, C.E.O.s, mayors, governors — changing their behavior in order to avoid the wrath of the government, that’s a sign that we’ve crossed the line into some form of authoritarianism,” said Steven Levitsky, a professor of government at Harvard and the co-author of the influential 2018 book “How Democracies Die.”_
**This is unacceptable in the United States of America.**
Peter — As Trump dismantles checks on his power and purges the government to fill every office with loyalists, more and more Americans are too afraid of retaliation to speak up.
**This descent into an un-American America must be stopped. We must regain Congressional power.**
I’m the ONLY Democratic Senator up for reelection in a state Trump won.
That means I’m MAGA’s #1 target for defeat.
**Help me beat them again, just like we did in 2021.**
[**Please contribute to my campaign today.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.lXrKfY&refcode=em251226-1-f&t=2&akid=1323%2E5691347%2ECShYmf)
Thank you for chipping in if you can.
\- Jon
**[DONATE](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.lXrKfY&refcode=em251226-1-f&t=3&akid=1323%2E5691347%2ECShYmf)**

Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Thank you for supporting Jon Ossoff. Email is a critical way to keep up with Jon and his work in the US Senate, but you can click [here](https://act.electjon.com/signup/fewer-emails-signup/?t=4&akid=1323%2E5691347%2ECShYmf) to sign up to receive fewer emails, or click [here](https://act.electjon.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=5&akid=1323%2E5691347%2ECShYmf) if you'd like to unsubscribe.

From the article: Fired federal workers who are worried about losing their homes ask not to be quoted by name. University presidents fearing that millions of dollars in federal funding could disappear are holding their fire. Chief executives alarmed by tariffs that could hurt their businesses are on mute. ... “When you see important societal actors — be it university presidents, media outlets, C.E.O.s, mayors, governors — changing their behavior in order to avoid the wrath of the government, that’s a sign that we’ve crossed the line into some form of authoritarianism,” said Steven Levitsky, a professor of government at Harvard and the co-author of the influential 2018 book “How Democracies Die.” This is unacceptable in the United States of America. Peter — As Trump dismantles checks on his power and purges the government to fill every office with loyalists, more and more Americans are too afraid of retaliation to speak up. This descent into an un-American America must be stopped. We must regain Congressional power. I’m the ONLY Democratic Senator up for reelection in a state Trump won. That means I’m MAGA’s #1 target for defeat. Help me beat them again, just like we did in 2021. Thank you for chipping in if you can. \- Jon Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate

name: Roy Cooper
subject: I’m grateful, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats
body:
#
Hi Peter — _I want to say thank you._
I know you’re probably tired of seeing all my requests for your donation coming through your inbox (I wish I didn’t have to send them so often, either). But in a race this close, every dollar counts. Emails like this one are our best way of earning your financial investment.
Although I will have to **[ask for your support](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98931?refcode=em20251226-9873&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9873%2E614464%2Egwzyls)** today as well — we have a huge end-of-quarter filing deadline five days away, after all — **my first priority today is to make sure you know how truly grateful I am.**
The outpouring of support our campaign has received so far has surpassed all my hopes. We are in a strong position in this toss-up race, and that’s all because of the momentum we’re building together.
You know, when I ran for public office the very first time in Nash County, NC, there were a lot of folks who had my back. Nash County is where I grew up. People there knew me and trusted me to do right by them. Without their help, I never could have won that first election.
**The stakes are even higher now, and I’m humbled to say that more and more of you are putting your faith and trust in us every day. You are the reason that I know that we can win the most competitive U.S. Senate race of 2026.**
In a race of national importance like this one, I don’t get as many chances to thank enough supporters personally, so it’s important to me to share my appreciation now. _Thank you._
Peter, if you’ve been thinking about making a donation to our campaign, it would mean a lot if you could do so right now. We set a big goal for this filing deadline — a million dollars. That’s what we need to raise to take on my far-right opponent, who has dark-money groups and PACs lined up and ready to spend _hundreds of millions_ against me. And after this deadline, we’ll need to file a public report, so falling short really isn’t an option. We can’t give the GOP that kind of opportunity to seize the momentum.
**[Please, split anything that fits in your budget between our campaign and State Democrats Committee. Your investment in our campaign is what will make it possible for us to succeed.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98931?refcode=em20251226-9873&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9873%2E614464%2Egwzyls)**
If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, your donation to Roy Cooper and State Democrats Committee will process instantly:
[Contribute $5](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98932?refcode=em20251226-9873&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=5&t=3&akid=9873%2E614464%2Egwzyls) [Contribute $15](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98932?refcode=em20251226-9873&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=15&t=4&akid=9873%2E614464%2Egwzyls) [Give another amount](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98931?refcode=em20251226-9873&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=5&akid=9873%2E614464%2Egwzyls)
---
Thank you very much,
Roy Cooper

Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats.com). Not authorized by any federal candidate or candidate committee.
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you. But, if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [you can do so here](https://act.statedemocrats.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails_state-democrats-committee/). If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.statedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe_state-democrats-committee/?t=6&akid=9873%2E614464%2Egwzyls).

A state built on faith, family, freedom, and the Second Amendment is being tested -- and its future will shape the country.

| | | [](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=c302f5b30b7a4601942ba08124224b47&_e=ADoBwGfAZSSihE9oDDviyzq7reKnOleY2Fp0pl7z0Za6rR9mSQSZ1R1k3q7MLTT12DO-3swqev916XCO4iAn6Wtxc41cXcNK1apx_tXsVw88W7QOh_sU9DaHgi4xGcRbOEZUKUq_qMXcWq6wCYCySibYR-3KvRfE0u8dQyOc0TnFJK1XpmPsaIRetB9blbdYvNO-AqObAyGpLk4rVKihtbJd8t5qD9vASWjBZZLxZg2uXPETMTCqyha4Z8eInhKI2RveMNEOZumTO43WpBVclTToCLXF2yENew6vuwWHy3BZ-01z1fSv84dVnfpSKZm1zekp0HUZFKY1IjflMHZGB0aVA8p580_aMfVVBVvEf5cq9ASrJ6e_5XbA1IxqKT54vxn7VmV8aPVVUulhmAprtEI0C1ZnR2ylP3YhlCVgByjdMfkAxFZn-T9Uz3qICCAJD17BYiGcEn3fmhPD0IbVLLk1ERPtH8bQYk-pQg6aFc2vj3x6rVlZYopm38Z4XWcDYvg2hhH6CvwNxzLFNOL5lofY2RiFhd1I8e6JjlaN549nSAbM4RCC2hKcS6weP4JV5V0swJufz1jsgFWnOc7aqzJyPPlRtXdmb9XuOw1csI4%3D)

---
| | If this is the first time you're hearing from me, let me introduce myself. My name is **Victor Marx** -- a husband, father, Marine Corps veteran, former pastor, and founder of an international humanitarian organization that has carried out more than 150 high-risk rescue missions around the world.I've spent my life stepping into the chaos most people run from, fighting for those who can't fight for themselves.
---
[](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=c302f5b30b7a4601942ba08124224b47&_e=ADoBwGfAZSSihE9oDDviyzq7reKnOleY2Fp0pl7z0Za6rR9mSQSZ1R1k3q7MLTT12DO-3swqev916XCO4iAn6Wtxc41cXcNK1apx_tXsVw88W7QOh_sU9DaHgi4xGcRbOEZUKUq_qMXcWq6wCYCySibYR-3KvRfE0u8dQyOc0TnFJK1XpmPsaIRetB9blbdYS6sYCAikqf1ovzHpo5Cn5g3GJvc8Z5-7p2K-2omtpWL5bFsNXfSgIGo1FjNdUqHzPoReSme58gEm6s2tZ4lwZpQdygpBTmrVDCKs9m5oXK_y7kWqIuznf_21YlDNy0f6DFKg8W2LRxf2KFZuT_FgAe6VHaZmWypIsgXFFx5BGBW6tgmL31Xvh2JJ99gC9AnLBMq--YkEmoCbKHPBrTS9vMgxN21xLtdq6Gn2Bv-jDu3GcRKIN0tP-HkZgh0IlHfs6g-d7rxIS7WkUI8NyAfuG_mxoFrlcfzFotSqTtXHlHL_gb1Y_XF2VbbCaJyvszy5rZwruk0Ng4C37Us0tHcuPFEAwtYySqBZh-va2PdIBhtVI4WBl8qAzZ6eZi1SsseCdCujdUNT5RBnwtzIXCypnvdxIFDghz05wX3Q9-ff-pw%3D)

**_I never expected to run for Governor of Colorado..._** But people I respected deeply -- _including Charlie and Erika Kirk_ -- believed in me and urged me to step forward because they saw what was happening here in our state.After Charlie was killed, Erika reminded me that he believed this was a fight I was meant to take on.**They were right, and I am now fully committed to winning this fight.**[_**Today, I am asking you to join me...**_](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=c302f5b30b7a4601942ba08124224b47&_e=ADoBwGfAZSSihE9oDDviyzq7reKnOleY2Fp0pl7z0Za6rR9mSQSZ1R1k3q7MLTT12DO-3swqev916XCO4iAn6Wtxc41cXcNK1apx_tXsVw88W7QOh_sU9DaHgi4xGcRbOEZUKUq_qMXcWq6wCYCySibYR-3KvRfE0u8dQyOc0TnFJK1XpmPsaIRetB9blbdYbh9xPApkz5kwn6TEskF4nCbyo6mI-Jdo1N5kWe0qfzF4Khkl6IUU_eiJY9Jz0uSiEbFTE9esQFSYcdJi4TA0-ulmUBMY3Wd4dFCeSGfHgRDrXWVQUEeHR0KMcuXVHHHhWpAgDt_kQI0vjyjRPNjDBFnEe0YS-f9sfsNF_N-FriliqTqdix96Lt190wtpVUV5Iu63yVY9KxbuhEco-QIABVNOwKPwfPNgHn4wXlq7F4Kr6wg_7EKPoHFes83GW8JMdGLdrE59MzQnIxvm1OwM_rsgMmpwxBCnOppaz7T44RM43rm0nkiRrrKNC8I7qho1d3hVEUIpGVcy31iu8-8YuZNHxqiMY8NkaZ5R78Btcy2sj6_1WqjxZKSjB1XxL2nyumzkcXKVjknodgOHu-WqFw41JPUwMTTOj3ySgDpTnzM%3D)

---
Because here's the truth most people outside our state don't realize: **_Colorado SHOULD still be a_**** _red_**** _state._** Colorado was built on independence, limited government, faith, family, and the unshakeable belief in the Second Amendment. Those values run deep in our history and in our people.But over time, the political establishment and a flood of left-wing ideas have overpowered the very values that made Colorado strong. We've watched our state shift from red, to purple, to blue -- not because the people changed, **but because leadership failed them.**_And that failure matters far beyond our borders._ Colorado has become a testing ground for policies that spread nationwide -- soft-on-crime agendas, government overreach, attacks on faith, restrictions on constitutional freedoms. **If we stop that tide**** _here_****, we stop it from sweeping across America.**
---
[ **JOIN VICTOR!**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=c302f5b30b7a4601942ba08124224b47&_e=ADoBwGfAZSSihE9oDDviyzq7reKnOleY2Fp0pl7z0Za6rR9mSQSZ1R1k3q7MLTT12DO-3swqev916XCO4iAn6Wtxc41cXcNK1apx_tXsVw88W7QOh_sU9DaHgi4xGcRbOEZUKUq_qMXcWq6wCYCySibYR-3KvRfE0u8dQyOc0TnFJK1XpmPsaIRetB9blbdYcuH3hPQUf06VYRWGlhN1l2x7OY7Np4yo27rTFtuTAD5CVpq1XGFQhauNui-_m1emsfzMdN57yq4QOZFfpwAA6LQBgbxQviD4_lGNjTBSJEZnUfEvX-Pla5qJM89KoyBceXWHxXv39nVlxAIYR0QIB9jI85qWNsFYl8Irg716vsnLYwm27vF2DpS2u8PQVerTelTPm7Nt3fTUouD2BCmY9epkDUmA4GeePg5B9Bl4OAB_J7L6crooY34wApqXhJyQXv394Lh1lT1_6QYBduIyO4r0z04H-V6LZcw-VKrZpWenzHgMEPvN5QkAwuh9QU7i2poLWWwqV2awgz3NaG1Go4fg-qhb_maboyV53i6LtwRkUBVjGERGhauEUQKZgeJJ_1EO67b9it0KlRkLWr3DCesnLg8hrYFKb1Kha360-YE%3D)
---
That's why this race is so important. **If Colorado flips back, the entire country will feel it.** It will prove that conservative, constitutional values aren't fading -- _they 're rising again!_ **Our state and country has too many politicians** , so instead, I'm asking you to stand with ME - a Marine, pastor, humanitarian, and Patriot - to ensure we have leaders who will fight to uphold and protect our values and freedoms. [_**Will you join me in this fight and invest in our campaign with a contribution of $25, $50 or even $100 today?**_](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=c302f5b30b7a4601942ba08124224b47&_e=ADoBwGfAZSSihE9oDDviyzq7reKnOleY2Fp0pl7z0Za6rR9mSQSZ1R1k3q7MLTT12DO-3swqev916XCO4iAn6Wtxc41cXcNK1apx_tXsVw88W7QOh_sU9DaHgi4xGcRbOEZUKUq_qMXcWq6wCYCySibYR-3KvRfE0u8dQyOc0TnFJK1XpmPsaIRetB9blbdY5afQVhLl-eIENDaxURnerIABYsvakKXLY1fgD-e1F6agN96-wvbEPCQMKI6FENW_0nZF1YcxCZIm83x4ATOQG7Acieaw2Elm7HkXdlyGRTrqqDnz1YQbP-ivVT82gc_DSC3-1UdsD9A4B3OgpE-vE51YYyQju7OSdc5WwhyHXrdnjvlAHxNxh8VexHGyjg-ZDApArULBmovMBXPdLaMojr8MF0Z6Kb94b86Yi8cPZarRM1o-gbH3oF_cR_t3YCmSMVs6-87kwEzGxGTy40JB_t8gUKzVwoZ7CyShtXWyDJ-iWpJO6uhGdodgMxkDT0OmNzEVx3z9ZOa4j1thiBxuf2CCGQ5oN0W5ScHYXxhtdc2a3rzCin_oFafc822d-0IUxkBTC-o7cZntlnXVb-OoPCrtEvtyS9WufCdhdMdjm70%3D)

[ **JOIN VICTOR!**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=c302f5b30b7a4601942ba08124224b47&_e=ADoBwGfAZSSihE9oDDviyzq7reKnOleY2Fp0pl7z0Za6rR9mSQSZ1R1k3q7MLTT12DO-3swqev916XCO4iAn6Wtxc41cXcNK1apx_tXsVw88W7QOh_sU9DaHgi4xGcRbOEZUKUq_qMXcWq6wCYCySibYR-3KvRfE0u8dQyOc0TnFJK1XpmPsaIRetB9blbdYn_a49D1GNeXSEq1o7GvIvBbz2CffRalB7KBni8B3WeOxMbmpPLi7H31uNFc9kDzVQ8XSyDReT9pG-uAC8_zFlZvlHnEP7YarkMu1NTclMiNeKhXt_0gHUDKXdRmB6Rs6YSU2eGfjxxnX9jigiQ7Wx68H2TsKqI4Zugf3o6wJRr_HHOUljT01t8JIdZ4746Fd9rqbNqwRYydjWWyVxJgTt9Zjb5NZnL-2RAo_ACS8kXM0VqZSNdf8BWSkLLcWi3G1sTNfr1a5C0rYRf41PaqUceFUHg0IxoSXL-bBq3eumW3gTImAhk8OvqGZbfSgY9yNwyThmYLP32Tvs_aK8lm0Wkr2mErcYnez2WJLhdq-v5NdyuA1LZFMlmFOjjB4wfAj1oLZJ6WNnpYMGefHgrZc639cvyl5WPKCygh2mJ4NLYs%3D)
---
This is our chance to send that message. This is our chance to restore a state that should never have been lost.And this is the moment every patriot -- in Colorado and across America -- should care about. If you're just learning about me today, I hope it's the beginning of something bigger. Because this movement isn't powered by mega-donors or coastal elites -- _**[it’s powered by people who are ready to take our state and country back.](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=c302f5b30b7a4601942ba08124224b47&_e=ADoBwGfAZSSihE9oDDviyzq7reKnOleY2Fp0pl7z0Za6rR9mSQSZ1R1k3q7MLTT12DO-3swqev916XCO4iAn6Wtxc41cXcNK1apx_tXsVw88W7QOh_sU9DaHgi4xGcRbOEZUKUq_qMXcWq6wCYCySibYR-3KvRfE0u8dQyOc0TnFJK1XpmPsaIRetB9blbdYS6sYCAikqf1ovzHpo5Cn5vszfsiP1f9F6vMonc1WLCQjFCgYVjvOWtZJDVBJTniNEwOzlKtqepUhk2kZwZkp2OzZyBIiFLgK3XGy-2PhrUsKn4fRyqgQo_98SugaB6DzHU2OkO9kSIasxUKUBvsM0iJNiq-v0jw1QfedPlkSb-XK1uBcv_UNrPDw788evIj3UtJpCqEoxJCDb31276Q5Z5avS95y_pyviYdZxLnxCvbiyuuS4l_kYo5-eCn8PJBxTQSsLjFG3_Ntbb7NWK3-JNuYFZcHw39vf9YizCgnAXmjfqCAo42Ob8uzhjaB5YseY7TgoDK4dAukF0piklCuIf1d6uxrtXNRAHsBLQae7pKqSPMA3q3if7-f_WlYyi9NCP1xrI8z1k5cShnMzmzixyL4VSTlbF_XYXf8enJEgo4%3D)**_ Thank you for standing with me!

For Colorado. For America.

clean_body: Marine, Pastor, Humanitarian, Patriot A state built on faith, family, freedom, and the Second Amendment is being tested -- and its future will shape the country. | | | | | Peter, | | If this is the first time you're hearing from me, let me introduce myself. My name is Victor Marx -- a husband, father, Marine Corps veteran, former pastor, and founder of an international humanitarian organization that has carried out more than 150 high-risk rescue missions around the world.I've spent my life stepping into the chaos most people run from, fighting for those who can't fight for themselves. I never expected to run for Governor of Colorado... But people I respected deeply -- including Charlie and Erika Kirk -- believed in me and urged me to step forward because they saw what was happening here in our state.After Charlie was killed, Erika reminded me that he believed this was a fight I was meant to take on.They were right, and I am now fully committed to winning this fight. Because here's the truth most people outside our state don't realize: Colorado SHOULD still be a red state. Colorado was built on independence, limited government, faith, family, and the unshakeable belief in the Second Amendment. Those values run deep in our history and in our people.But over time, the political establishment and a flood of left-wing ideas have overpowered the very values that made Colorado strong. We've watched our state shift from red, to purple, to blue -- not because the people changed, but because leadership failed them.And that failure matters far beyond our borders. Colorado has become a testing ground for policies that spread nationwide -- soft-on-crime agendas, government overreach, attacks on faith, restrictions on constitutional freedoms. If we stop that tide here, we stop it from sweeping across America. That's why this race is so important. If Colorado flips back, the entire country will feel it. It will prove that conservative, constitutional values aren't fading -- they 're rising again! Our state and country has too many politicians , so instead, I'm asking you to stand with ME - a Marine, pastor, humanitarian, and Patriot - to ensure we have leaders who will fight to uphold and protect our values and freedoms. This is our chance to send that message. This is our chance to restore a state that should never have been lost.And this is the moment every patriot -- in Colorado and across America -- should care about. If you're just learning about me today, I hope it's the beginning of something bigger. Because this movement isn't powered by mega-donors or coastal elites -- Thank you for standing with me! For Colorado. For America. Victor Marx Conservative for Governor of Colorado | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- | | PAID FOR BY VICTOR MARX FOR GOVERNOR Registered Agent: Gregory Carlson | | P.O. Box 156 Florence, CO 81226

name: Abdul for U.S. Senate
subject: All eyes will be on this report

body:
[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1001&akid=1472%2E251025%2Erd7mR- ]Abdul for US Senate [ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1002&akid=1472%2E251025%2Erd7mR- ]Abdul for US Senate

Hi Peter,

We have an actual, immovable deadline on December 31st, and we want to
take a minute to explain what it is and why it matters so much to our
campaign’s success.

Each quarter, every federal campaign is required to file a report with the
Federal Election Commission (FEC). The FEC collects and publishes every
campaign finance report online for the public to access.

Here’s what our profile looks like on their website:

[ A ]A screenshot of Abdul's profile on the FEC's website

Once our report is published, anyone can see it.

Including the media.

Including our opponents.

And including corporate PACs and AIPAC.

They’ll look at our numbers, compare them to others, and decide which
campaigns are real threats — and which ones they think they can write off.

This December 31st filing is our final report of the year, heading into a
high-stakes election year with a competitive primary and a Trump-backed
MAGA Republican waiting on the other side.

That’s why reporting strong numbers matters. It helps us fend off attacks
before they start and shows everyone that our people-powered campaign has
real momentum.

And because Abdul is the only candidate in this race who has never taken
money from corporate PACs or AIPAC, we’re fully relying on folks like you
to help us meet our $250,000 goal. Right now, we’re 84% of the way there.

[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/507?c0=em-ms-aes-20251226-1-ak.jvOo7L&amount=10&refcode=em20251226-1461&t=1004&akid=1472%2E251025%2Erd7mR- ]If you’re able, will you pitch in $10 today to help make
sure our FEC report shows undeniable strength?

[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/507?c0=em-ms-aes-20251226-1-ak.jvOo7L&amount=10&refcode=em20251226-1461&t=1023&akid=1472%2E251025%2Erd7mR- ]Donate $10 ››

All eyes will be on this report. Your contribution helps make sure it
tells the full story of the movement behind Abdul.

Thank you,

Team Abdul

clean_body: [ - ]Abdul for US Senate [ - ]Abdul for US Senate Hi Peter, We have an actual, immovable deadline on December 31st, and we want to take a minute to explain what it is and why it matters so much to our campaign’s success. Each quarter, every federal campaign is required to file a report with the Federal Election Commission (FEC). The FEC collects and publishes every campaign finance report online for the public to access. Here’s what our profile looks like on their website: [ A ]A screenshot of Abdul's profile on the FEC's website Once our report is published, anyone can see it. Including the media. Including our opponents. And including corporate PACs and AIPAC. They’ll look at our numbers, compare them to others, and decide which campaigns are real threats — and which ones they think they can write off. This December 31st filing is our final report of the year, heading into a high-stakes election year with a competitive primary and a Trump-backed MAGA Republican waiting on the other side. That’s why reporting strong numbers matters. It helps us fend off attacks before they start and shows everyone that our people-powered campaign has real momentum. And because Abdul is the only candidate in this race who has never taken money from corporate PACs or AIPAC, we’re fully relying on folks like you to help us meet our $250,000 goal. Right now, we’re 84% of the way there. [ - ]If you’re able, will you pitch in $10 today to help make sure our FEC report shows undeniable strength? [ - ]Donate $10 ›› All eyes will be on this report. Your contribution helps make sure it tells the full story of the movement behind Abdul. Thank you, Team Abdul

name: Jasmine Crockett
subject: It’s official: I’m running for Senate.
body:
Y’all, it’s Jasmine.
I’m reaching out with big news, and I wanted you to hear it from me first: I’m officially running for the United States Senate here in Texas.
Let me be clear: With our current wannabe dictator in the White House, I’m not launching this campaign to play nice. I’m doing it to fight — because Texas deserves someone who doesn’t back down when the bullies come for her. It deserves someone who fights back.
But to win this race, we’ll have to take on Donald Trump, the Texas Republican political machine, and all of their shady special interest donors. And to be blunt, that won’t come cheap.
That’s why I’m asking you to become one of my founding donors as we launch this historic grassroots campaign. I’m counting on your support now more than ever before so that we can hit the ground running and finally flip Texas blue. Will you answer the call by splitting a donation between my campaign and Real Texas? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?refcode=120825_1]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Jasmine Crockett and The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately:
DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?express_lane=true&refcode=120825_1&amount=5]
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?express_lane=true&refcode=120825_1&amount=10]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?express_lane=true&refcode=120825_1&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?express_lane=true&refcode=120825_1&amount=100]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?express_lane=true&refcode=120825_1&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?refcode=120825_1&amount=25]
Let me be clear: When there’s a fight worth having, I don’t flinch. I don’t shrink. I don’t back down.
Just ask the communities I’ve fought for as a civil-rights attorney, the defendants I’ve represented as a public defender, and the people of Dallas that I serve in Congress. I’m a lifelong fighter for justice.
So Donald Trump can run his mouth all he wants. All his attacks show is that he’s terrified of what I represent — and what I’m bringing to the Senate when we win this race: truth, accountability, and a future where power answers to the people.
If you believe that insults won’t silence us — if you believe in a Texas that stands for justice, equity, and real representation — I need you. We’re launching a campaign to change the game. But real campaigns need real support. That’s why I’d be so grateful if you could pitch in $10, $25, or more to help us hit the ground running. Can I count on you to split a donation between my campaign and Real Texas ? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?refcode=120825_1]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Jasmine Crockett and The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately:
DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?express_lane=true&refcode=120825_1&amount=5]
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?express_lane=true&refcode=120825_1&amount=10]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?express_lane=true&refcode=120825_1&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?express_lane=true&refcode=120825_1&amount=100]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?express_lane=true&refcode=120825_1&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?refcode=120825_1&amount=25]
Thank you,
Jasmine

Married couples may together give a total of $10,000 per calendar year. Federal political action committees (PACs) can contribute up to $5,000 per calendar year. The first $5,000 contributed in a calendar year from federally permissible sources will be deposited in The Real Texas PAC's contribution account. name: Andy, Digital Director (via Team Cooper)
subject: Looking at this so Roy doesn’t have to
body:
The campaign underbelly.
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2365?source=&t=1&akid=209%2E124072%2Eb27ehp)
Hey Peter — nice to meet you! My name's Andy Volosky, and I'm Roy's Digital Director. I know Roy's been reaching out over the last few days about our end-of-quarter deadline, and I wanted to jump in with a different perspective from my side of the campaign. **I'm the one tracking every online donation that comes through, and right now, I need to flag something _really_ important.** In five days, we’ll reach our fundraising deadline, which means we’ll have to file a public report with the Federal Election Commission. When we file, _everyone_ will see our online fundraising numbers — our opponent, the GOP, political analysts, reporters. So here's where things stand as of today: We’re a little more than halfway to our $1 million online goal, and we have five days to close the gap. **[Peter, if you’re all-in on electing Roy to the Senate — and I know you are because you’re reading my email the day after Christmas — _I need you to chip in a few bucks ASAP._](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3248?t=7&akid=209%2E124072%2Eb27ehp)** | [DONATE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3248?t=8&akid=209%2E124072%2Eb27ehp)
---
I know it’s a weird time to be asking, but online donations are the clearest sign of a campaign’s strength. They show whether everyday people are invested in this race, or whether we're getting drowned out by the mega-donors bankrolling Michael Whatley.
And when the GOP sees our report, they'll make a calculation: _Is Roy's support strong enough that we need to pour even more into defeating him? Is there a weakness we can exploit?_
We can't give them an opening. Not in a toss-up race. Not when NC is the key to the Senate majority. **If you've been thinking about chipping in, this is the moment.**
**[So I’m asking again: Can Roy count on your donation today? Chip in anything that works for you to help us put up big numbers when our report goes public next month.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3248?t=9&akid=209%2E124072%2Eb27ehp)**
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
[DONATE $5](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3249?t=10&akid=209%2E124072%2Eb27ehp)
---
[DONATE $10](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3250?t=11&akid=209%2E124072%2Eb27ehp)
---
[DONATE $25](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3251?t=12&akid=209%2E124072%2Eb27ehp)
---
[DONATE $50](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3252?t=13&akid=209%2E124072%2Eb27ehp)
---
[DONATE $100](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3253?t=14&akid=209%2E124072%2Eb27ehp)
---
[DONATE ANOTHER AMOUNT](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3254?t=15&akid=209%2E124072%2Eb27ehp)
---
Thanks so much. Let's finish this year strong.
Andy Volosky
Digital Director
Roy Cooper for U.S. Senate
* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=2&akid=209%2E124072%2Eb27ehp)**
[DONATE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=3&akid=209%2E124072%2Eb27ehp)
---
[DONATE MONTHLY](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1915?source=&t=4&akid=209%2E124072%2Eb27ehp)
[SHOP OUR MERCH STORE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2133?t=5&akid=209%2E124072%2Eb27ehp)
[info@roycooper.com](mailto:info@roycooper.com) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.roycooper.com/go/825?t=6&akid=209%2E124072%2Eb27ehp)
Cooper Victory Fund
PO Box 1190
Raleigh, NC 27602
Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Cooper for North Carolina, DSCC, and North Carolina Democratic Party — Federal.
---
Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible.
The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.
Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email address, with a check, or with a spouse — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.
By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution.

[You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time.](http://act.roycooper.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=209%2E124072%2Eb27ehp)
If you wish to receive fewer messages from us, [submit this form to let us know.](https://act.roycooper.com/signup/i-want-less-emails-cooper-north-carolina/?t=16&akid=209%2E124072%2Eb27ehp)

subject: The Top 5 Reasons to Give in 2025

Peter,

We’re just five days away from our year-end deadline, and we’re still only halfway to our December fundraising goal.

**But we have some good news: Every gift made right now will be DOUBLED up to $50,000 thanks to a year-end match.**

Here are the top 5 reasons your gift today matters more than ever:

**1\. Emerge women win at rates no one else can match.**
First-time Emerge candidates are six times more likely to win than the national average.

**2\. Emerge delivers real results in important battlegrounds.**
In 2025, nearly 300 alums ran in 20 states and 223 won — a 77% win rate — including historic victories in Virginia that built a Democratic trifecta.

**3\. Emerge is building the bench to help Democrats flip the House.**
29 Emerge alums are already running for Congress in 2026 — the largest group we’ve ever had.

**4\. Emerge builds long-term Democratic power.**
With 7,000 women trained and 1,200 serving in office, we’re changing who leads in this country.

**5\. Your gift is doubled — but only for five more days.**
This match expires at midnight on December 31. Every dollar you give now fuels recruitment and training for the women who will run and win in 2026.

How can we do all of this? Because of members like you!

[_**Will you make a MATCHED contribution today so we can finish December strong and hit our year-end goal?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122004927/591278387/1143593133?refcode=BLU_EM_EMRG_2025-12-26_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImYyMjg5NGVjLTZlZTItZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=V-kwZwrj67Bfm_HFhv13YMewzostVE2re7jKpt3CFXQ=)
