name: Team Taylor
subject: We’ll get straight to the point
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Taylor for Justice
body:
Our campaign is facing a major public fundraising deadline on December 31.
# [](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/18?refcode=em20251226-946&t=1&akid=956%2E294397%2EzqIY78)
We’ll get straight to the point:
**Our campaign is facing a major public fundraising deadline on December 31.**
Every dollar we’ve raised to that point will be reported publicly for right-wing special interests to see.
We know they’re already preparing to spend millions of dollars to try and buy a seat on the Wisconsin Supreme Court, and this report will shape how aggressively they move next.
That’s why we set a digital fundraising goal of raising **$115,000** before December 31.
Reaching this goal means we can start the new year ready to compete with whatever the far right throws at us.
Grassroots donations give us the resources to run ads, reach voters across Wisconsin, and push back when right-wing special interests target Judge Chris Taylor.
**But the only way we’ll meet this goal is if people like you step up and contribute.** [**If you’re able to, pitch in $10 to help us meet our $115,000 goal before the December 31 deadline:**](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/31?c0=em_ms_jct_20251226_1_ak.7gen3i&amount=10&refcode=em20251226-946&t=3&akid=956%2E294397%2EzqIY78)
**[Donate $10 ››](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/31?c0=em_ms_jct_20251226_1_ak.7gen3i&amount=10&refcode=em20251226-946&t=22&akid=956%2E294397%2EzqIY78)**
With the election happening next April, what we raise right now plays a major role in whether we’re able to reach the voters who will decide this race.
Thank you for taking action.
— Team Taylor
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_jct_footer?refcode=em20251226-946&t=23&refcode2=956_294397_zqIY78&akid=956%2E294397%2EzqIY78)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=24&akid=956%2E294397%2EzqIY78)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=25&akid=956%2E294397%2EzqIY78)

Email us: [info@chrisforjustice.com](info@chrisforjustice.com)
Paid for by Chris Taylor for Justice
PO Box 1921
Madison, WI 53701

We have to tell you what’s happened:

FIRST:
Democrats delivered HUGE wins in Virginia, New Jersey, New York, California, Pennsylvania, and Georgia.

THEN:
Donald Trump announced a $1.9 BILLION warchest to CRUSH Democrats’ momentum.

NOW:
Gavin Newsom is speaking out:

Gavin Newsom: "We will fight for our democracy. And we will win." [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP12/1/91305/images/20251119_em_fr_b2_hmp_main_6x.png]

Democrats finally have the momentum to defeat Trump – and Gavin Newsom knows that we cannot allow this critical moment to go to waste.

If we waste this critical moment, we may never have another opportunity to stop Donald Trump and his MAGA House Majority from completely destroying our Democracy.

That’s how urgent this crossroads is.

Top Democrats know that if we fail to step up in this critical moment, we may lose our one chance to defeat Donald Trump and bring his disastrous MAGA House Majority crumbling down. That’s why they’ve stepped up to unlock a HUGE 7X-MATCH on every contribution we receive from this email – but only for the next 14 hours. If we’re going to have any hope of fighting back against Donald Trump’s $1.9 BILLION warchest, we need every Democrat reading this to fight back. subject: A massive erasure of fair representation for people of color
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FAIR FIGHT ACTION
body:
Here’s what can happen at the state level if the Supreme Court guts Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122005364/591279202/1793776944?refcode=emm_ffa_20251226_T1_VRAreportNOR_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYzJhYTAzOS02ZmUyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CUb7yVpZdrkm6IvHC5iZ065bLXc9zIfbPFV5ievAnpk=&emci=eacc5a57-01e2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=3c2aa039-6fe2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=1037277)

| | Our latest report exposes a new dimension of the threat posed by a pending Supreme Court decision. The right-wing justices seem likely to gut Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act, our central safeguard against racially discriminatory redistricting. We’d already found that such a ruling could secure 19 new safe Republican seats in the U.S. House. Enough to cement long-term control even if Democrats win the popular vote. But the damage wouldn’t end there. **[Our new report](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122005366/591279209/325450178?refcode=emm_ffa_20251226_T1_VRAreportNOR_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYzJhYTAzOS02ZmUyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CUb7yVpZdrkm6IvHC5iZ065bLXc9zIfbPFV5ievAnpk=&emci=eacc5a57-01e2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=3c2aa039-6fe2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=1037277), co-released this month with Black Voters Matter Fund, details the additional, sweeping consequences at the state legislative level. As a result of the ruling, lawmakers could:**
* **Eliminate approximately 191 state legislative seats** currently held by Democrats across 10 Southern states — the vast majority of these are Black representatives for majority-minority districts
* **Decimate fair representation and minority political power** at the state level
* Lock in GOP supermajorities for a generation
* Block progress on healthcare, civil rights, education, criminal justice reform, and more

**This would represent a massive erasure of fair representation for people of color, silencing millions of voices and undoing the gains in political power that communities of color fought for and built over generations.** [**Click here to read the report, and share it with five people you know. Tell them what’s at stake: core American principles of fair representation and protecting people from discrimination.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122005368/591279215/325450178?refcode=emm_ffa_20251226_T1_VRAreportNOR_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYzJhYTAzOS02ZmUyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CUb7yVpZdrkm6IvHC5iZ065bLXc9zIfbPFV5ievAnpk=&emci=eacc5a57-01e2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=3c2aa039-6fe2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=1037277) We’re raising the alarm with this new report right now because the window to protect Black and Latino political power and fair representation is closing. The fight ahead requires immediate action, sustained organizing, and strategic investment. As soon as the Supreme Court releases a decision gutting Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act (if they do), state lawmakers will likely move quickly to draw new maps. But with this team’s support, we’ll more than match their urgency. Our pro-democracy movement will:
* Fight back against any proposed racially discriminatory maps
* Work with partners to redraw fair maps wherever possible to offset map rigging
* Advocate for pro-democracy reforms at the state and federal level to ensure fair representation
* Push Congress to investigate and expose unfair maps that silence minority voices
* Demand transparency and accountability from the Supreme Court

**Through grassroots mobilization, public education, coalition building, and public pressure, we can keep up the fight for fair representation, and for our democracy.** **And we must.** [**Power our movement to protect free and fair with a donation today.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122005370/591279222/-1580673587?refcode=emm_ffa_20251226_T1_VRAreportNOR_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYzJhYTAzOS02ZmUyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CUb7yVpZdrkm6IvHC5iZ065bLXc9zIfbPFV5ievAnpk=) _If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically:_ | [Chip in $5](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122005372/591279229/-1580673587?amount=5&express_lane=true&refcode=emm_ffa_20251226_T1_VRAreportNOR_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYzJhYTAzOS02ZmUyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CUb7yVpZdrkm6IvHC5iZ065bLXc9zIfbPFV5ievAnpk=) | [Chip in $25](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122005374/591279232/-1580673587?amount=25&express_lane=true&refcode=emm_ffa_20251226_T1_VRAreportNOR_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYzJhYTAzOS02ZmUyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CUb7yVpZdrkm6IvHC5iZ065bLXc9zIfbPFV5ievAnpk=)

name: Bushra Amiwala
subject: I was offered $56,500
body:
| | | | | [](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001h63NmlOzrFcMgsW0W6ab1YrjNBc6TwCwR6zvVIdxVONM8V3uO_e1HMAyENnsrgpPpXNkkxxyo1BMVO_o6YimCZlAP7MMgDdRpgqexLdRjNHRs-dXa7FL7UBOVjMb6njlE-9vYmi0pLntz0pwfIeQT-vGGqvpQF6I7QNhP8LqPSk8i3UZMALLmpCuLL-MCi-LE-58gN4lASNSGbG6dTklTybKAy0lKT5G&c=R7WorA1kUDbXd2xD0FJmIFTapvicWOtyefNtq8rxf62dHKz73xxUKw==&ch=ZJsL3JOIklYxSGYErJ8sJbF5pSpLDL_f8bq8o_l3pCVJiKKIDGdNkA==)
---
|
Peter,
When I first ran for office in 2018, I was a young candidate running to be a Cook County Commissioner--trying to break into a political system that wasn't built for people like me.
I was offered a contribution of $56,500 from a corporate PAC.
For a first-time candidate running for a county-level seat, that is a staggering amount of money. That is the type of check that can swing an election. It's the kind of money that propels careers and secures victories.
But I didn't even have to think about it.
I refused the check.
---
| | [](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001h63NmlOzrFcMgsW0W6ab1YrjNBc6TwCwR6zvVIdxVONM8V3uO_e1HMAyENnsrgpPpXNkkxxyo1BMVO_o6YimCZlAP7MMgDdRpgqexLdRjNHRs-dXa7FL7UBOVjMb6njlE-9vYmi0pLntz0pwfIeQT-vGGqvpQF6I7QNhP8LqPSk8i3UZMALLmpCuLL-MCi-LE-58gN4lASN_dhQmRH1rB7nyVmctWkHA&c=R7WorA1kUDbXd2xD0FJmIFTapvicWOtyefNtq8rxf62dHKz73xxUKw==&ch=ZJsL3JOIklYxSGYErJ8sJbF5pSpLDL_f8bq8o_l3pCVJiKKIDGdNkA==)
---
| Now, in my campaign to be the next Congresswoman for IL-9, these same special interests and corporations are back--circling this open seat like vultures, looking for any candidate willing to take their money and do their bidding in Congress.
I made a choice back then to rely on people, not corporate PACs. It's a harder road, but it's the only one worth taking.
Groups like AIPAC are geared to spend up to seven figures to try and buy this seat. It's going to take everything we have to counter their spending, and because I won't take their money, I rely on grassroots supporters like you to fuel this movement.
[So I am asking you today: If you believe we need to get big money out of politics and elect leaders who can't be bought, will you chip in $25, $10 or whatever you can afford to help send this movement from the streets of Chicago to the halls of Congress?](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001h63NmlOzrFcMgsW0W6ab1YrjNBc6TwCwR6zvVIdxVONM8V3uO_e1HMAyENnsrgpP_-nkEa1Xh5WvZrhjxxwPiJJdQdYViILwLtr2Ov6AMb9S4OvXRKGa0ZLMVfTA61bA9ZoDDiCD4oTkHb16Ywy1a8LgIVs2c5rwtbB48hVoy4BfjNRjlic0ga3tVoUG5RsA7JdSyvNwRDTXjlch1ZVnFHuPz_sisVXSehfE7ArzPI0=&c=R7WorA1kUDbXd2xD0FJmIFTapvicWOtyefNtq8rxf62dHKz73xxUKw==&ch=ZJsL3JOIklYxSGYErJ8sJbF5pSpLDL_f8bq8o_l3pCVJiKKIDGdNkA==)

clean_body: | | | | | == | Peter, When I first ran for office in 2018, I was a young candidate running to be a Cook County Commissioner--trying to break into a political system that wasn't built for people like me. I was offered a contribution of $56,500 from a corporate PAC. For a first-time candidate running for a county-level seat, that is a staggering amount of money. That is the type of check that can swing an election. It's the kind of money that propels careers and secures victories. But I didn't even have to think about it. I refused the check. | | == | Now, in my campaign to be the next Congresswoman for IL-9, these same special interests and corporations are back--circling this open seat like vultures, looking for any candidate willing to take their money and do their bidding in Congress. I made a choice back then to rely on people, not corporate PACs. It's a harder road, but it's the only one worth taking. Groups like AIPAC are geared to spend up to seven figures to try and buy this seat. It's going to take everything we have to counter their spending, and because I won't take their money, I rely on grassroots supporters like you to fuel this movement. == | If you've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: | | == | | == | | == | | == | | == | | == | == | That corporate check was a golden ticket to an easy career, but it came with a price tag: my soul. I'd rather fight tooth and nail for every grassroots dollar than owe a single favor to special interests. | == Bushra Amiwala Democrat for IL-9 | | == | | | | == | Mail checks to: Bushra for Congress PO Box 911 Skokie, IL 60076 | | == | | | | Bushra for Congress | PO Box 911 | Skokie, IL 60076 US | |

name: Ro Khanna
subject: ENDORSEMENT ALERT—Why I’m supporting Dylan Hewitt for US Congress
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ro for Congress
body:
https://rokhanna.com [https://rokhanna.com]
Dear Peter,
I’m proud to endorse Dylan Hewitt to represent New York’s 21st Congressional District.
Before I tell you why I’m excited to support Dylan, let me tell you why it matters.
Races like this don’t just determine who represents a district; they help determine who controls Congress. NY-21 will be a hard-to-win race. But with the right candidate, like Dylan, it is exactly the kind of seat Democrats can flip in our race to regain control of the House in 2026. Can I count on you to help provide the resources to win? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rk-dylan-hewitt-split?refcode=20251226]
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rk-dylan-hewitt-split?refcode=20251226]
Now, let me introduce you to Dylan.
Dylan is a seventh-generation Upstate New Yorker , born and raised in South Glens Falls. For more than two centuries, his family has lived, worked, and run small businesses across the North Country. Public service isn’t a slogan in Dylan’s family, it’s a way of life.
His father spent decades as an elementary school principal. His mother dedicated her career to helping families as a social worker. His grandfather, a World War II veteran and longtime public school teacher, believed deeply in looking out for your neighbors, a lesson Dylan learned early and never forgot.
Dylan carried those values with him into public service at the highest levels. As a White House trade official, he protected American workers and farmers, challenged greedy corporations, and strengthened our national security. But no matter how far his work took him, he never forgot where he came from – or who he was fighting for.
Now, after nearly 30 years of calling the district home, Dylan is stepping up to serve the North Country.
He’s running to lower costs for working families, fight for a living wage, universal childcare and healthcare , and make sure every generation has a fair shot at success. And he’s doing it with the same values his family has lived by for generations: resilience, service, and community.
But he can’t flip this red district without our help.
Will you rush to split a contribution between Dylan Hewitt and my campaign today, before the December 31 deadline? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rk-dylan-hewitt-split?refcode=20251226]
Your support right now will help build a grassroots campaign strong enough to win and ensure the North Country finally has the representation it deserves.
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rk-dylan-hewitt-split?refcode=20251226]
Thank you for your commitment to help flip NY-21 blue and win back the House in 2026.
In solidarity,
Ro
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rk-dylan-hewitt-split?refcode=20251226]

body:
Happy Holidays! The holidays are a time for reflection, gratitude and looking ahead to the year to come. As I spend this time with my family, I'm thinking about all of you and the incredible support you've shown for Democratic candidates this year. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122009465/591291089/-510661250?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.26_Holiday-Message_B1_F1_S1_C1_90AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxOTkxZTBmNi03M2UyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tD--gSx1XYoR30MRFuYF8lzJOfOQ9OEYUQ3lJAw765o=) This season reminds me why I got into public service in the first place — to fight for families like yours and mine. To make sure every child has the opportunities they deserve, every worker earns a fair wage and every voice is heard in Washington. **We have a real opportunity in 2026 to flip the Senate and get back to delivering results for the American people.** We can defend health care access and SNAP benefits, protect Social Security and Medicare and build an economy that works for everyone — not just those at the top — _but only if we all step up to get it done._ None of this will be easy. It will take all of us working together, starting right now. That's why I'm reaching out during the holidays – because there are no days off if we’re going to defeat the Republican extremist majority. We’re aiming to raise $100,000 before our final public fundraising deadline of the year on December 31st, and if we’re going to hit that ambitious goal, we’re going to need Democrats like you to help out. Even $10 will help us recruit strong candidates, organize in key states and build the grassroots power to take back the Senate majority. **[_Can I count on you to chip in? Please rush anything you can here before our final public fundraising deadline in 5 days:_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122009468/591291098/-510661250?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.26_Holiday-Message_B1_F1_S1_C1_90AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxOTkxZTBmNi03M2UyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tD--gSx1XYoR30MRFuYF8lzJOfOQ9OEYUQ3lJAw765o=)** _If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [ DONATE $10 >> ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122009471/591291108/-510661250?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.26_Holiday-Message_B1_F1_S1_C1_90AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxOTkxZTBmNi03M2UyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tD--gSx1XYoR30MRFuYF8lzJOfOQ9OEYUQ3lJAw765o=)

Team, there is **a bill that would give President Donald Trump broad powers to target his political foes** which passed in the U.S. House of Representatives last Congress and is likely to come up again.The bill, last known as H.R. 9495, would empower the Treasury secretary to unilaterally designate any nonprofit as a “terrorist supporting organization” and revoke its tax-exempt status, effectively killing the group. **The bill could give presidential administrations a tool to crack down on organizations for political reasons. ** “ _Authoritarianism is not born overnight — it creeps in_ ,” Rep. Lloyd Doggett said on the House floor. “ _A tyrant tightens his grip not just by seizing power, but when he demands new powers and when those who can stop him willingly cede and bend to his will_.” “ _A sixth grader would know this is unconstitutional_ ,” added Rep. Jamie Raskin. Unconstitutional and authoritarian or not, this bill has passed the House once and has a good chance of passing both houses of Congress now that opponents of voting rights have total control of our government. **That's why we need to start working now** to prevent this bill from ever becoming law. **[Will you chip in with $25 or whatever you can to help us stop this bill and protect the ability of people-powered groups to advocate for our rights?>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122010345/591293317/1498593520?refcode=122625_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5MjE1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjJlM2EyNzFkLTc0ZTItZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=fv-rmwwJBtr-F_w35IYslJgfXEgL-LbZV5F1kmaY71s=)**

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.

Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities. clean_body: Truthout: House Passes Chilling “Nonprofit Killer” Bill| | | | | = Team, there is a bill that would give President Donald Trump broad powers to target his political foes which passed in the U.S. House of Representatives last Congress and is likely to come up again.The bill, last known as H.R. 9495, would empower the Treasury secretary to unilaterally designate any nonprofit as a “terrorist supporting organization” and revoke its tax-exempt status, effectively killing the group. The bill could give presidential administrations a tool to crack down on organizations for political reasons. “ Authoritarianism is not born overnight — it creeps in ,” Rep. Lloyd Doggett said on the House floor. “ A tyrant tightens his grip not just by seizing power, but when he demands new powers and when those who can stop him willingly cede and bend to his will.” “ A sixth grader would know this is unconstitutional ,” added Rep. Jamie Raskin. Unconstitutional and authoritarian or not, this bill has passed the House once and has a good chance of passing both houses of Congress now that opponents of voting rights have total control of our government. That's why we need to start working now to prevent this bill from ever becoming law. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = | = | | = | = | = | | | | | | | ---|---|---|--- Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country. Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities. = = Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). We’ve seen just how powerful our movement can be when we stand together to each do our part — and 2025 has been full of examples of ordinary people rising up:
* The volunteers in Missouri who gathered hundreds of thousands of petition signatures to put their gerrymandered map on the ballot for rejection.

* The Indiana voters who made clear to lawmakers they were against their attempted mid-decade map redraw.

* The millions of voters in Virginia, Pennsylvania, and beyond who voted for pro-democracy candidates up and down the ballot.

But we’ve got our work cut out for us in the months ahead — and that’s why we’re asking people in California to step up with a donation today.

Come January, we need to have the resources necessary to take on anti-democracy forces in up to five states — and we simply can’t fall short. That’s why we’re looking for just TWENTY-FOUR supporters to donate from California before tomorrow night. = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = We’ve seen just how powerful our movement can be when we stand together to each do our part — and 2025 has been full of examples of ordinary people rising up: The volunteers in Missouri who gathered hundreds of thousands of petition signatures to put their gerrymandered map on the ballot for rejection. The millions of voters in Virginia, Pennsylvania, and beyond who voted for pro-democracy candidates up and down the ballot. subject: Who likes Trump more than President Biden?! [please confirm >>]
disclaimer_text: paid for and provided by End Citizens United Nonfederal, P
body:
| Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23743%2E4449941%2EZvCXhz) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23743%2E4449941%2EZvCXhz) [**Did you see Trump’s LATEST lies about President Biden? **](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71061?t=3&akid=23743%2E4449941%2EZvCXhz) [](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71061?t=4&akid=23743%2E4449941%2EZvCXhz) **REMINDER: in only 11 months, Donald Trump has...**
* **DESTROYED** Social Security programs that Joe Biden expanded,
* **THREATEND WAR** on multiple American Cities,
* **SUGGESTED HANGING** sitting Democratic Lawmakers,
* **FIRED** thousands of federal workers for no reasons,
* And so much more!

I don’t know about you, friend, but we miss President Biden! [**5,OOO RESPONSES NEEDED BEFORE MIDNIGHT: Who is a better President? →**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71061?t=5&akid=23743%2E4449941%2EZvCXhz) [](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71061?t=6&akid=23743%2E4449941%2EZvCXhz)
| | | [JOE BIDEN ➞](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71061?t=7&akid=23743%2E4449941%2EZvCXhz)
---
| [DONALD TRUMP ➞](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71061?t=8&akid=23743%2E4449941%2EZvCXhz)
---
[UNSURE ➞](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71061?t=9&akid=23743%2E4449941%2EZvCXhz)
---
We _can’t_ let Donald Trump continue to slander Joe Biden’s legacy...

Remember friend, under President Joe Biden...
* The FIRST Black woman was nominated to the Supreme Court,
* Our world alliances were STRENGTHENED,
* The first meaningful gun legislation was passed in OVER 30 YEARS,
* Voting rights were INCREASED across the country,
* And so much more!

**So, we NEED to make sure Joe Biden knows how much we miss him!**
[**Who do you think is the better president?**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71061?t=10&akid=23743%2E4449941%2EZvCXhz)
| | [JOE BIDEN ➞](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71061?t=11&akid=23743%2E4449941%2EZvCXhz)
---
| [DONALD TRUMP ➞](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71061?t=12&akid=23743%2E4449941%2EZvCXhz)
---
[UNSURE ➞](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71061?t=13&akid=23743%2E4449941%2EZvCXhz)
---
**We need your response so we can send Donald Trump crying!**

PLEASE answer before tomorrow!

Thanks,

Fight For Reform

DESTROYED Social Security programs that Joe Biden expanded, THREATEND WAR on multiple American Cities, SUGGESTED HANGING sitting Democratic Lawmakers, FIRED thousands of federal workers for no reasons, And so much more! I don’t know about you, friend, but we miss President Biden! | | | | We can’t let Donald Trump continue to slander Joe Biden’s legacy... Remember friend, under President Joe Biden... The FIRST Black woman was nominated to the Supreme Court, Our world alliances were STRENGTHENED, The first meaningful gun legislation was passed in OVER 30 YEARS, Voting rights were INCREASED across the country, And so much more! So, we NEED to make sure Joe Biden knows how much we miss him! | | | We need your response so we can send Donald Trump crying! PLEASE answer before tomorrow! Thanks, Fight For Reform | This message was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com | | Paid political advertisement paid for and provided by End Citizens United Nonfederal, P.O. Box 66005, Washington, D.C. 20035. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Contributions to End Citizens United Non-Federal are not tax deductible. Contributions to End Citizens United Non-federal will be used in End Citizens United Non-federal’s sole discretion and are not being earmarked for use in any particular state or election.

body:
I want to reach out today to thank you for your tremendous support for House Democrats. | | | | [ ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvYDpWCXLG6Uy7oDuCG4hFmEn1sONHETAl3vR46E0D8rF20PM_AvHzxLPPol-03SEch6cPLD9EwLXI4e06co5IOvqUUhyl2-0IlXWTfK1Upz7eVPMSIfxOvAstiIwx03PlpXiVxZ4VTKGF7lSCHi6cR20J9kkVw6a9rZUuYwgpahBSvwFogeL6AYzZUFoywyH24v9OfUUAKt9EbOxmJm1noFQbUBDA7j20jl7lFmkxfOxMWcbxSkajI2bS-W098sQbeWt2w4cdw041SMmX4BCK9ysrDY1Gdk4lnH9IqkZvUhiqnmUM0MIGkw5c-7xU3jE7Vfo8F-y6-7Tr8YEGyfQ7M5P-gj84ZqMUOPZGcVufJLCOaBwtuA6of8Rg9za7TBR0/4mr/zwgjn8IHTZKAE1N40n0EsA/h0/h001.urVPIxACU8rcCOjKJt6UsW0MP5-KTIvp0hanJ9tDlt8)
---
Friend, I want to reach out today to thank you for your tremendous support for House Democrats. It would mean a great deal if you could take the time to read this entire message.
**Together, we are building a grassroots movement that will pave the way for a historic victory next year.**
All across the country, supporters like you are chipping in to fight back against extreme MAGA Republicans. So many of you are making donations, volunteering your time and spreading our winning message to your friends and neighbors.
I’m proud that our movement includes:
– Veterans dedicated to protecting our freedoms
– Union members fighting for good-paying jobs
– Parents and grandparents who want a better future for their loved ones
****[**I’m reaching out to our strongest supporters to personally ask you to split a $10 gift between NV Dems and the DCCC to keep us on track to reclaim the House and elect Democrats across the country. I know we have what it takes to win. We can’t do it without your continued support >>**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvYDpWCXLG6Uy7oDuCG4hFmEn1sONHETAl3vR46E0D8rF20PM_AvHzxLPPol-03SEch6cPLD9EwLXI4e06co5IO1TixW0RrV8hmFc4_zQNvHCbBEW78rxJ8Cpb7cjv0-KZUNiaFjwEP3uWcJQrFXGhHCIED7JCFBZ0BiJr9ss1IrFyGkbrO-kmy7K_kJz2c3Mo1qZX4Tn26cbTiGlln6_qDAGHBjQUEzYE6MgJv9why_1Sdr47cRW4o_kuE5Rjn0r-syHw-XqoFaLY3os5Kmql9ozXFK6HHciS5fwQiRXWAwMtzTIMx8V0q5_sQ0tle0lHRvlGuWCYUmgr1lFKiIh5fdZpejQBU6Oub3caaPoDxYpCdWjJJfvxyFuLZIPKe65AumM1TMyKMTbskSKtmEk8o/4mr/zwgjn8IHTZKAE1N40n0EsA/h1/h001.0wVVKFQMz2yJ0Do37fSYEoR1KtTZGur9oXxtOJQPF0w)****
**House Democrats are working hard to lower the high cost of living, fix our broken healthcare system and clean up Republican corruption. We will get it done with your help.**
We are at a pivotal moment in our nation’s history.
Instead of meeting the needs of everyday Americans, Donald Trump and the GOP are prioritizing tax breaks for billionaires, slashing healthcare and ripping food from the mouths of children.
Their behavior is despicable, friend.
We cannot let Republicans get away with this betrayal of the American people.
That is why we must flip the three seats we need to reclaim the House majority and end this national nightmare once and for all.
This election is going to be extremely competitive and incredibly expensive, and we cannot win without you by our side.
****[**It would mean so much to me if you’d split a donation between NV Dems and the DCCC to help reclaim the House majority, defend our democracy from Republican extremists and put a much-needed check on Donald Trump >>**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvYDpWCXLG6Uy7oDuCG4hFmEn1sONHETAl3vR46E0D8rF20PM_AvHzxLPPol-03SEch6cPLD9EwLXI4e06co5IOhaKH914HGxqE4H2h0hmt2zZR26ze6qIquvhKdVYTXB-5o3i6HYw1XNIG_XbB8XyVwgDoawJ7AuWOHYO5RMPeL3YSeLms4RxtYK3rwivpuynny95Z_Gk65ngra9nwQ6f8ftYUXP8nT5nhOad3s5SwpwLRodN2_I9MbPUkgHUGXV89IXEolf2WG-8X_f-oma9WZzfpWbwNqlxsZ0bXGILjZpW1TnLKwVbqgeedeAG2kuY7Smc4aMuuwm_tbuiLM5o9yI4r1G-EawSM_ZVtZytP-tZICapGPiIJVMZtwy5AC1c/4mr/zwgjn8IHTZKAE1N40n0EsA/h2/h001.b5x5fHlV9obKMV5obSeN2Tmf37Er1ytH6Oogdf6sCVI)****
****

clean_body: I want to reach out today to thank you for your tremendous support for House Democrats. | | | | Friend, I want to reach out today to thank you for your tremendous support for House Democrats. It would mean a great deal if you could take the time to read this entire message. Together, we are building a grassroots movement that will pave the way for a historic victory next year. All across the country, supporters like you are chipping in to fight back against extreme MAGA Republicans. So many of you are making donations, volunteering your time and spreading our winning message to your friends and neighbors. I’m proud that our movement includes: – Veterans dedicated to protecting our freedoms – Union members fighting for good-paying jobs – Parents and grandparents who want a better future for their loved ones ** House Democrats are working hard to lower the high cost of living, fix our broken healthcare system and clean up Republican corruption. We will get it done with your help. We are at a pivotal moment in our nation’s history. Instead of meeting the needs of everyday Americans, Donald Trump and the GOP are prioritizing tax breaks for billionaires, slashing healthcare and ripping food from the mouths of children. Their behavior is despicable, friend. We cannot let Republicans get away with this betrayal of the American people. That is why we must flip the three seats we need to reclaim the House majority and end this national nightmare once and for all. This election is going to be extremely competitive and incredibly expensive, and we cannot win without you by our side. ** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | ---|---|--- | | Keep the faith, Hakeem The Nevada State Democratic Party fights for progress in the Silver State. We believe that we are stronger together than on our own, and that America succeeds when everyone gets a fair shot, everyone does their fair share, and everyone plays by the same rules. | | Paid for by the Nevada State Democratic Party, NVDEMS.com, not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. clean_body: You’re sending a statement when you wear this Tell the world you're standing for faith, family, and freedom. | | Patriot, We are Charlie Kirk. That's the rally cry of millions of Americans -- and the message they carry with their "We are Charlie Kirk" wristbands. We want you to have one. To join the movement carrying Charlie's legacy forward. When you give any amount today, we 'll send you your wristband -- a bold symbol of the mission we carry on. This isn't just a wristband, Patriot. It's a symbol. Of conviction. Of courage. Of the movement Charlie built. When you wear it, you 're telling the world you stand for America. For faith. For family. For freedom. And for the vision Charlie ignited. Your donation fuels the next generation of leaders who will defend these values and never surrender. Give today and we 'll put this powerful symbol in your hands as our thank-you. For Charlie, The Turning Point USA Team 4940 East Beverly Road Phoenix, Arizona 85044

name: Chris Pappas for Senate
subject: “Musk diving into the 2026 midterms”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
body:
Elon Musk is diving into the 2026 midterms for the GOP.
# [](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1&akid=4233%2E582294%2Ehg_D4X)
**“Elon Musk has begun funding the GOP's House and Senate campaigns for the 2026 midterms.”**
That’s the quote from Axios we were worried we might see. Far-right billionaire money is going to come pouring in to defeat Democrats in battleground races — and New Hampshire is the GOP’s top target.
[](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/221?c0=em_ms_cp_20251226_1.Tka_eG&refcode=em20251226-1&t=2&akid=4233%2E582294%2Ehg_D4X)
---
**[We need to mobilize our grassroots supporters now to be able to fight back. Rush a donation of any amount so we can keep New Hampshire blue »](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251226_1.jyMW_s&refcode=em20251226-1&t=3&akid=4233%2E582294%2Ehg_D4X)**
**[RUSH A DONATION »](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251226_1.jyMW_s&refcode=em20251226-1&t=4&akid=4233%2E582294%2Ehg_D4X)**
The only way we’ll stand up to the millions wielded by the national GOP, super PACs, and their billionaire allies is with a big grassroots team behind Chris.
If everyone reading this email chipped in just $5, we’d hit our end-of-year goal immediately. If everyone steps up today, we’ll have the power to stop Elon Musk in his tracks.
**[So what do you say? Will you rush your very first donation to Chris Pappas so we can fight back?](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251226_1.jyMW_s&refcode=em20251226-1&t=5&akid=4233%2E582294%2Ehg_D4X)**
Let’s show them NH is not for sale.
Thank you,
Chris Pappas for Senate
**New Hampshire is poised to be one of the closest Senate races in the country in 2026. Will you chip in today to help Chris stop the Republicans from flipping the Granite State red and expanding their Senate majority?**
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_footer?refcode=em20251226-1&t=6&refcode2=4233_582294_hg_D4X&akid=4233%2E582294%2Ehg_D4X)**

name: Jane Fonda
subject: I've been reflecting on 2025
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee
body:
I couldn't be prouder of what we accomplished together.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122012948/591300861/1328794144?refcode=ea.d0fe76e8-76e2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.d0fe76e8-76e2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQwZmU3NmU4LTc2ZTItZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=bx1ztG3ICcVpEsRENKtW245XbNEX0e_BSL2hQqaV59s=&emci=4c118797-8adf-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&emdi=d0fe76e8-76e2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=4823105)
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122012949/591300862/2105508926?refcode=ea.d0fe76e8-76e2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.d0fe76e8-76e2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQwZmU3NmU4LTc2ZTItZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=bx1ztG3ICcVpEsRENKtW245XbNEX0e_BSL2hQqaV59s=) I've been reflecting on all we've accomplished together this year, and I couldn't be prouder of the victories we delivered. We swept every race we invested in across Virginia.
We helped elect the Commonwealth’s first Muslim woman to statewide office.
We flipped the Luzerne County Council in Pennsylvania from a 10–1 GOP supermajority in 2023 to a pro-environment majority.
And in places like Georgia and New Jersey, we proved — once again — that when we organize early and stay determined, _nothing_ is out of reach. This is what grassroots power looks like. And our work is far from over. 2026 will be a make-or-break year for our planet, and quite frankly, our democracy. Trump and his fossil fuel cronies are already mobilizing to expand their grip on Congress and silence anyone who stands in their way. We can't afford to slow down — _especially with one of our JanePAC-endorsed climate champions facing a critical runoff in Texas next month._ Protecting this seat is essential, and it’s our first chance in 2026 to show that our climate champions will not be intimidated. That's why we’ve set one final goal of 2025: **Raise $30,000 by midnight on December 31. These resources will ensure we hit the ground running in the new year, defending key seats, expanding the map, and electing bold climate champions in places others have written off as “unwinnable.”** **[This is one of your final opportunities to act in 2025. clean_body: I couldn't be prouder of what we accomplished together. | = I've been reflecting on all we've accomplished together this year, and I couldn't be prouder of the victories we delivered. We swept every race we invested in across Virginia. We helped elect the Commonwealth’s first Muslim woman to statewide office. We flipped the Luzerne County Council in Pennsylvania from a 10–1 GOP supermajority in 2023 to a pro-environment majority. And in places like Georgia and New Jersey, we proved — once again — that when we organize early and stay determined, nothing is out of reach. This is what grassroots power looks like. And our work is far from over. 2026 will be a make-or-break year for our planet, and quite frankly, our democracy. Trump and his fossil fuel cronies are already mobilizing to expand their grip on Congress and silence anyone who stands in their way. We can't afford to slow down — especially with one of our JanePAC-endorsed climate champions facing a critical runoff in Texas next month. Protecting this seat is essential, and it’s our first chance in 2026 to show that our climate champions will not be intimidated. That's why we’ve set one final goal of 2025: Raise $30,000 by midnight on December 31. These resources will ensure we hit the ground running in the new year, defending key seats, expanding the map, and electing bold climate champions in places others have written off as “unwinnable.” = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = xx Jane | = | = = Follow along: | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. body:
I need you with me, Friend. | | | Friend, I never planned on running for office again. But I see what’s happening in Washington – and I can’t stay on the sidelines. **I’m jumping in this fight: I’m running for Senate in 2026.** _Why?_ Because Ohioans are working harder than ever, and they’re still falling behind. _Why?_ Because __ wages are flat. _Why?_ Because costs are rising. _Why?_ Because the people in power are only looking out for their rich friends and corporate interests. No one is fighting for Ohio workers and families in the Senate. But with your support, we can change that. We can build a country where workers share in the wealth they create, where hard work pays off, and where everyone who works hard can afford a decent life. This won’t be easy – and with Ohio being one of the most competitive Senate races in the country, we know there are many fights ahead. But if we stand together, we will win. ****[**I need you with me, Friend: Will you chip in $10 split with Able Dems right now to help me flip Ohio’s Senate seat and stand up for workers across the country?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtHtWNDtXR0WReOmqZcCOXOzlTtz-Osg_YPdrY6QhRg_ItkwHBtzfcZtOCKbttRWejVBQ1prhIIM2molTvXy48LqiugoEUcI9u6V1cajb0f4di2DGzbMxgv9s1Q7BspSs5U6j5uSxAC2Y7hDRy0KNZ1hgyi5kB4wRYFRC2mKUsUGN7-MSkHJFGnQzrHdluch1GwXUd7IO6MND_KnBSABubyx1uUwbN_nspnkoZ8UG3NhsJyLq8-AvSBsQ92Y9iTLFlE5P1GzFw-nekdpYZTEym2Ey_5_1KWd_hlJXrGeN6IeA/4mr/zPZsU1dPTjKQQnah3XHczQ/h0/h001.V4JAKfXRPGYXHAFBUuIXUG9jxcNftlzCNLXsxkRBprY)**** **** | [DONATE](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtHtWNDtXR0WReOmqZcCOXOzlTtz-Osg_YPdrY6QhRg_FTNhO6Y_VfeqlRYUf_j6IILIM2Ij2UWIn5fuwLwe4IPkDYpRf9L3a3dUCpNv532GMzXmOKvo8crjEBxAhnmgzuPykYKdOgvOqDEezTCWTwY6V2V9kPWc_QhNzmV-E-MxbjWzYLkATrrfgMF5uhyyhnq1eBWYLTynClujlPgbwi_zDxAmX8DVuenSaQ8XGOdmKg3qguFW3bXVeVMoIxxF79m3mlUAFPVPmdvSe3OymM9_1stja9MSNJf3dy2doOydw/4mr/zPZsU1dPTjKQQnah3XHczQ/h1/h001.JdTnFBAg0o4pRRBYiPHPvbiSmsyhRQkMtcxN_Plf9bw)

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---
Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.

Get the full truth [**HERE**](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=0a8af15dce2a40cba1b6ae6d6f571c55&_e=lip3emvGwvoafJaGUe5VPUOefuFfhchznhwv_V4s5pQlo19tZqDuZfezCC15ECao1SmD-eq4ACIH6_I1pqUvpQ5gg32wCVpCQvTZYsXEQ6wxB-bjcom6TZMAMpwJliDIyLIyAO-9hZ_ez_yTSAA-5buqJ_qTxBlXYEOkmvxLMjSxt1wtW0MQNo3i3VZGryE1rAtUyivYNhjrlykqqFaWoC-RB23mDIdZnr5KknLjRNvRMnotwBM7IKBvl3RBkEdC5tp9rZY8tsm8ANYquWY75gY_2yd1HpVYLUdrLWxmD-gNscwqHn2E6oERUaI9fpy9BlIw3zgL-Gj9C-u-WRzPA-DbG1lyYVJbTnY4_mASBUk0bplQUHte6rsE6UfqVYCrv2Z_o3_z7yr6VklpUDCec4DM2zIYq9MyHvsLM3HRhwDoNpgar7AQv38VwlHdyjQMZDyE5UmJymSR6kM-1cwagEfcADhlOt7X2rcZ06k7T4h6hiW3fmsmiQAx__xE8qkSaW7TjTC1W1WbYGehsqrB5gpRaPCfTjb9UJ3Wh6vkj60hH39vEzUOKa6El9ScjO9DD4fr1N7H7gS5K8G3lmI4sQ%3D%3D) now while it’s still publicly available.
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: Neil Armstrong reported a strange encounter he had in space during a post-mission debrief.| | | | December 26 | | | | | What Neil Armstrong REALLY saw on the moon [TERRIFYING] | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. | You won’t see in any history book... But after becoming the first man to set foot on the moon... Neil Armstrong reported a he had in space during a post-mission debrief. And he wasn’t the only astronaut to experience it. NASA did their best to keep it secret... But now, unearthed is blowing the lid off of everything. is only seconds long, but it will chill you to the bone. P.S. Of all the moon landing conspiracies, could be the most incredible. Get the full truth now while it’s still publicly available. Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: James Carville
subject: If I had any hair, I’d be ripping it out!!!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
If we don’t get off our butts and help Chris Pappas right now, we can kiss the Senate majority goodbye.
Folks, it’s James Carville.
I’ve been around the block a few times and worked on a heck of a lot of campaigns. So believe me when I tell you: **This ain’t good.**
John Sununu — yeah, _those Sununus_ — just jumped into the U.S. Senate race in New Hampshire. And now the political eggheads have DOWNGRADED Democrats’ chances of winning there.
**[💥Before you read another word, split $10 or whatever you can between Chris Pappas and Heartland Patriots to help Chris fight back right now.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98902?refcode=em20251226-9869&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9869%2E614464%2EVqEUuC)**
Let me tell you what this means: **If we don’t get off our butts and help Chris Pappas right now, we can kiss our chances of winning back the Senate majority goodbye.**
This isn’t some back-bench, no-name guy we’re up against. **Sununu’s won a Senate seat before.**
He’s got name ID, deep pockets, and Wall Street’s dark money machine ready to drown us in attack ads. This is the far-right’s big play, y’all — they know if they take New Hampshire, Democrats have _no viable path_ to flipping the Senate.
Chris Pappas is the real deal. I mean it. He’s won the toughest district in New Hampshire four damn times, and he knows how to bring folks together. But he cannot — **_cannot_** — do this alone.
So here’s what I need from you:
**[Split a contribution between Chris Pappas and Heartland Patriots today. $10, $25, whatever you can spare. Because if we let the GOP buy this seat for Sununu, we’re gonna be stuck with Republican control for years to come.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98902?refcode=em20251226-9869&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9869%2E614464%2EVqEUuC)**
**[Split a donation now »](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98902?refcode=em20251226-9869&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=9869%2E614464%2EVqEUuC)**
I’m mad as HELL that this race just shifted in the wrong direction — but that means it’s time to get serious. Chris is the only one who can hold this seat, but he needs a hell of a lot more firepower to fend off what’s coming.
Let’s show these Republican bigshots that we’re not scared — and we sure as hell ain’t backing down.
**[Give what you can right now.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98902?refcode=em20251226-9869&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=9869%2E614464%2EVqEUuC)** Let’s win this damn thing and flip control of the Senate.
— James Carville
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98902?refcode=em20251226-9869&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=5&akid=9869%2E614464%2EVqEUuC)

body:
We're already more than halfway through December, and I have some bad news. |
---

Fellow American,

We're already more than halfway through December, and I have some bad news.

My campaign fell $2,045 short of its mid-month fundraising goal. We cannot afford to fall short of our final fundraising goal for 2025. We cannot start Election Year 2026 in the "red."

My campaign fell $2,045 short of its mid-month fundraising goal. We cannot afford to fall short of our final fundraising goal for 2025. We cannot start Election Year 2026 in the "red." So I have a personal request for you. Thanks, Steve Daines Steve Daines is a fifth-generation Montanan, life-long sportsman and father of four serving the people of Montana as their Senator. Steve is committed to always putting Montana first as he works to bring real change to Washington and fights for more jobs, less government and common sense solutions to get our country back on track. subject: Let me level with you, Derek

body:
I want to explain something

|

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122015136/591308177/-52083569?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_EOY1_251226_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNWYwOTc1Yi03Y2UyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Zp0JgBT4u_apJhnJPD-C_qPTTAZeiXG4EqlCIkFzcA4=) Derek,

[_**In a moment, I’m going to ask you to chip in to my reelection campaign before my critical end-of-year fundraising deadline**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122015139/591308179/-52083569?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_EOY1_251226_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNWYwOTc1Yi03Y2UyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Zp0JgBT4u_apJhnJPD-C_qPTTAZeiXG4EqlCIkFzcA4=), but before I do, I want to explain what’s at stake:

**Trump’s first term was bad, Derek, but frankly, his second has been cataclysmic.**

Trump and his acolytes have ripped apart the federal workforce, degraded our intelligence community at every turn, increased the cost of living through reckless tariffs, ripped health care from millions of Americans and skyrocketed premiums for millions more, and jeopardized our national security... all while handing tax breaks to the wealthiest Americans.

Yet every step of the way, my Republican colleagues in Congress bend the knee to Trump, even when they know his administration is hurting their constituents.

**But here's the thing: 2026 is when Democrats flip control of Congress – and Virginia is where it starts.**

Next November, Virginians will decide between protecting this seat and taking back the Senate for Democrats so we can _finally_ put a check on this corrupt, dangerous Trump administration... or flipping this seat red – giving Trump _another_ yes-man in the U.S. Senate and crushing any chance of a Democratic Senate majority.

What we do today – just days before our end-of-year deadline – will make all the difference as my campaign team and I work to earn every vote next year.

[_**That's why I'm asking: can you chip in before our end-of-year deadline to help us reach our $250,000 goal so we can kick off the election year with the resources we need to win, take back the Trump and his acolytes have ripped apart the federal workforce, degraded our intelligence community at every turn, increased the cost of living through reckless tariffs, ripped health care from millions of Americans and skyrocketed premiums for millions more, and jeopardized our national security... all while handing tax breaks to the wealthiest Americans. Yet every step of the way, my Republican colleagues in Congress bend the knee to Trump, even when they know his administration is hurting their constituents. But here's the thing: 2026 is when Democrats flip control of Congress – and Virginia is where it starts. Next November, Virginians will decide between protecting this seat and taking back the Senate for Democrats so we can finally put a check on this corrupt, dangerous Trump administration... or flipping this seat red – giving Trump another yes-man in the U.S. Senate and crushing any chance of a Democratic Senate majority. name: James Carville
subject: UNGODLY spending against James Talarico
body:
[ https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251226-1-ak&t=1001&akid=343%2E1347274%2EEhKsXn ]James Carville

Listen up, Peter: We have to get
serious about Texas.

If you want a Democratic Senate majority, then you need to care about this
race.

You know I’m not one for BS. The cold hard truth is, if anyone can flip
Texas, it’s James Talarico.

But not if we fail to support him. [ https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251226-1-ak&t=1002&akid=343%2E1347274%2EEhKsXn ]Please, before his FEC deadline,
contribute $5 or whatever you can to his campaign. This is
make-or-break.

I’ve worked on tough races — big wins and devastating defeats — for over
50 years. I know what it takes to win in “red states” where people think
you can’t elect a Democrat.

Let me say loud and clear: James has what it takes. He’s one of Democrats’
best fighters and communicators — and most importantly, he knows how to
WIN.

His message breaks through, and he delivers results — just like he did
when he capped insulin co-pays and lowered housing costs in the Texas
legislature.

But super PACs will spend UNGODLY sums — hundreds of millions — to defeat
James and take Texas off the map for Democrats. Unless we do all we can
before the FEC deadline, we’re handing this seat straight to a corrupt
MAGA politician and the Senate majority to national Republicans.

What happens now is up to you. Ask yourself: Do you care about Texas?
About the Senate majority?

[ https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251226-1-ak&t=1003&akid=343%2E1347274%2EEhKsXn ]If the answer is yes, then James needs you to give $5 or
whatever you can before the FEC deadline. This is the most important
moment of his campaign so far. When James reports his fundraising numbers,
we want it to be absolutely clear that the people are with him and ready
to win this race.

If you've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution
instantly.

[ https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/506?c0=jt-em-launch.3s2pDp&refcode=em251226-1-ak&t=1022&akid=343%2E1347274%2EEhKsXn ]Donate $5

# **Trump says US military destroyed drug ship facility**
President Donald Trump **[told WABC radio host John Catsimatidis](https://substack.com/redirect/c92fd741-9db4-4169-bb36-da10c0a49176?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** on Friday that the US military destroyed "a big plant" or "facility where the ships come from" two nights earlier, claiming "every time we knock out a boat, we save 25,000 American lives" as part of a months-long campaign targeting suspected drug-running vessels originating in Venezuela.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c92fd741-9db4-4169-bb36-da10c0a49176?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Strike location remains unspecified:** Trump did not say precisely where the strike took place, including whether it was on the Venezuelan mainland, during the interview discussing the ongoing US military campaign against drug trafficking operations.

* * *
# **Obama Foundation defends windowless design of presidential library**
Obama Foundation Deputy Director Kim Patterson explained that the 225-foot-tall gray building on Chicago's South Side has few windows because **["sunlight is just not a friend to the artwork and the artifacts,"](https://substack.com/redirect/098be508-791c-4d67-9aa1-e8ac1733b39a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** while the building's shape was "meant to mimic four hands coming together to show the importance of our collective action."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/628f52bb-024a-4098-a6ef-9f4b5e26b1c5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Project faces years of criticism and legal challenges:** **[Construction began](https://substack.com/redirect/628f52bb-024a-4098-a6ef-9f4b5e26b1c5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** in 2021 after the project faced pushback including a lawsuit filed in 2018 accusing the city of illegally transferring parkland to the Obama Foundation (which ended in 2022), protests, and criticism of its brutalist architectural style, with some locals dubbing it "The Obamalisk."
* **Costs balloon to $850 million ahead of 2026 opening:** The project's cost has grown from an estimated $500 million in 2017 to roughly $850 million in 2025, with the Obama Presidential Center scheduled to open in June 2026, though Patterson noted community input led to changes like building the parking garage underground to avoid blocking sunlight to local gardens.

Costs balloon to $850 million ahead of 2026 opening: The project's cost has grown from an estimated $500 million in 2017 to roughly $850 million in 2025, with the Obama Presidential Center scheduled to open in June 2026, though Patterson noted community input led to changes like building the parking garage underground to avoid blocking sunlight to local gardens. Must Watch: Asking TPUSA girls who 's more attractive, Conservative men or Liberal men? * You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Politibrawl 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?
