| [](https://substack.com/redirect/cf376ecc-05ff-43c2-8b94-79cb406e0f50?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Another Rush Hour on I-95 - Where are the Interstate Trucks?
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=182636245&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyNjM2MjQ1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjY3NzAyNDksImV4cCI6MTc2OTM2MjI0OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE0NTQ4NTMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.gPMpQzT9vyaSj4fr2E_OQdJxYhOCFDwv-lkh298ncic)
[Pledge your support](https://substack..bsoPzy-2csf7dgpYxBzQjPKUX1RygPg_JnLy0LakDY0?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=182636245)
**Congrats Connecticut - We are Number 1 at Something Other Than Pizza - Traffic Congestion**
According to [INRIX, Inc.](https://substack.com/redirect/e8b68f14-8c56-4f25-b70a-f3862a27f230?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a global leader in transportation data and analytics, their 2024 Global Traffic Scorecard Ranks the Stamford/Westport Corridor as the Worst Corridor in the Nation. The average minutes lost per day is 38 minutes or 151 hours a year - Almost 5 weeks of work.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d9e0672b-71cb-45be-a534-140944d35ac5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[Source ](https://substack.com/redirect/241fb5e3-dfe7-4799-8cbf-d60df987cc17?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)(https://inrix.com/press-releases/2024-global-traffic-scorecard-us/)
Interstate 95 and the Merritt Parkway during the morning and evening rush hour are so overcrowded that traffic barely flows. _**They are also jammed up at weird hours as well, especially Saturdays.**_
At 4 PM or 5PM it can take over on hour to travel between Greenwich and Westport. Based on my own personal experience, it takes less time to travel from East 61st Street in Manhattan to Greenwich than it takes to travel from Greenwich to Westport. This is an unacceptable outcome.
**FUN FACT: The Average Use of I-95 In Connecticut is About 11 Miles**
The only solution (given current constraints) is to widen I-95 from Stamford to at least Bridgeport. Contrary to the opinions of some, I-95 is a commuter road in Fairfield County and most people do not use it as an "Interstate".
Having driven on I-95 from Co-Op City in the Bronx to the Greenwich border during rush hour, I have almost always never seen traffic on the road. However, once you enter Greenwich there is a significant slowdown. If it were all due to interstate trucks, the road would be congested in the Bronx. Unlike the NJ Turnpike, which is primarily an interstate highway with some exits between 2-10 miles apart, I-95 has exits that are extremely close together. There are 4 exits in Greenwich, 4 exits in Stamford, 2 exits in Darien, 4 Exits in Norwalk, 2 exits in Westport, 1 exit in Southport and 6 exits in Fairfield.
**Whenever I drive during rush hour, there are far more cars than trucks.**
The Merritt Parkway which has no trucks is equally congested during the rush hour commutes.
_**The amount of time and the economic consequences of all this wasted time more than justifies road expansion. Cars in stop and go traffic generate more carbon emissions than ones that are moving at a more efficient speed.**_
If I-95 were widened, it would move faster. Some cars would shift from the Merritt to I-95 and that road would also move faster.
Despite the efforts of Green Energy Extremists to eliminate gas powered cars, Americans (and now American car manufacturers without subsidies) have rejected it. Manufacturers are shifting away from producing EVs and instead producing hybrids and gas powered vehicles. **Hybrids are the only economical vehicles with an EV component.**
If you truly want to stop people from commuting on I-95, the solution is for the State of Connecticut to change its policies to promote, rather that deter, economic growth. People in Fairfield County drive on I-95 to jobs in New York, but they choose to live in Connecticut.
People who live in Connecticut and work in New York pay most of their state income tax to New York. If those jobs were in Connecticut, they would be paying their income tax to Connecticut.
**Stamford - The 6th Boro of CT**
Stamford is practically, the 6th Boro of NYC given express trains and short commute times. This further exacerbates the problem, as many Stamford residents are renters and not owners. Thus, no equity is built in CT while all the income tax goes across the border to NY. As renters, they do not directly pay property tax and are not as incentivized to stand up for local issues when the mil rates increase as they do not feel it the same way.
_**Local economic growth and job creation, and wider roads will reduce commuting time on I-95 and bring more income tax revenue, from both the corporations and their employees to Connecticut.**_
**Government must work for the people not the extremists. This is the Green Connecticut needs - Keeping the tax dollars in Connecticut.**
| A guest post by| [Jonathan Goldstein](https://substack.com/@jonathangoldstein81?utm_campaign=guest_post_bio&utm_medium=email)Business Consultant, Political Consultant, Campaign Manager, Construction Consultant and Corporate Attorney. clean_body: Average Distance Travelled is 11 Miles | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more Goldstein For Congress - Please Subscribe and also visit us at www.goldsteinforcongress.com Average Distance Travelled is 11 Miles | | and | Dec 26 | | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- Another Rush Hour on I-95 - Where are the Interstate Trucks? Congrats Connecticut - We are Number 1 at Something Other Than Pizza - Traffic Congestion According to , a global leader in transportation data and analytics, their 2024 Global Traffic Scorecard Ranks the Stamford/Westport Corridor as the Worst Corridor in the Nation. The average minutes lost per day is 38 minutes or 151 hours a year - Almost 5 weeks of work. | | ---|---|--- ) Interstate 95 and the Merritt Parkway during the morning and evening rush hour are so overcrowded that traffic barely flows. They are also jammed up at weird hours as well, especially Saturdays. At 4 PM or 5PM it can take over on hour to travel between Greenwich and Westport. Based on my own personal experience, it takes less time to travel from East 61st Street in Manhattan to Greenwich than it takes to travel from Greenwich to Westport. This is an unacceptable outcome. FUN FACT: The Average Use of I-95 In Connecticut is About 11 Miles The only solution (given current constraints) is to widen I-95 from Stamford to at least Bridgeport. Contrary to the opinions of some, I-95 is a commuter road in Fairfield County and most people do not use it as an "Interstate". Having driven on I-95 from Co-Op City in the Bronx to the Greenwich border during rush hour, I have almost always never seen traffic on the road. However, once you enter Greenwich there is a significant slowdown. If it were all due to interstate trucks, the road would be congested in the Bronx. Unlike the NJ Turnpike, which is primarily an interstate highway with some exits between 2-10 miles apart, I-95 has exits that are extremely close together. There are 4 exits in Greenwich, 4 exits in Stamford, 2 exits in Darien, 4 Exits in Norwalk, 2 exits in Westport, 1 exit in Southport and 6 exits in Fairfield. Whenever I drive during rush hour, there are far more cars than trucks. The Merritt Parkway which has no trucks is equally congested during the rush hour commutes. The amount of time and the economic consequences of all this wasted time more than justifies road expansion. Cars in stop and go traffic generate more carbon emissions than ones that are moving at a more efficient speed. If I-95 were widened, it would move faster. Some cars would shift from the Merritt to I-95 and that road would also move faster. Despite the efforts of Green Energy Extremists to eliminate gas powered cars, Americans (and now American car manufacturers without subsidies) have rejected it. Manufacturers are shifting away from producing EVs and instead producing hybrids and gas powered vehicles. Hybrids are the only economical vehicles with an EV component. If you truly want to stop people from commuting on I-95, the solution is for the State of Connecticut to change its policies to promote, rather that deter, economic growth. People in Fairfield County drive on I-95 to jobs in New York, but they choose to live in Connecticut. People who live in Connecticut and work in New York pay most of their state income tax to New York. If those jobs were in Connecticut, they would be paying their income tax to Connecticut. Stamford - The 6th Boro of CT Stamford is practically, the 6th Boro of NYC given express trains and short commute times. This further exacerbates the problem, as many Stamford residents are renters and not owners. Thus, no equity is built in CT while all the income tax goes across the border to NY. As renters, they do not directly pay property tax and are not as incentivized to stand up for local issues when the mil rates increase as they do not feel it the same way. Local economic growth and job creation, and wider roads will reduce commuting time on I-95 and bring more income tax revenue, from both the corporations and their employees to Connecticut. Government must work for the people not the extremists. This is the Green Connecticut needs - Keeping the tax dollars in Connecticut.** | A guest post by| Consultant, Political Consultant, Campaign Manager, Construction Consultant and Corporate Attorney. Jonathan served as the Campaign Manager for Dr. Michael Goldstein - CT-04 The Goldstein Substack is free today. name: Team Jayapal
subject: What are they hiding? Who are they protecting?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
Survivors have spent almost two decades demanding accountability
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20251226-5745-footer&t=1&akid=5745%2E420207%2EGLPrTX)
Peter,
In response to Congress forcing their hand, the Trump administration recently released a batch of Epstein files and photos, but it has raised more questions than answers.
Hundreds of pages of evidence were completely blacked out, they failed to release _all_ of the files as was required by law, and the release came across like a document dump designed to overwhelm the public.
**That’s not transparency, Peter. That’s more obstruction.**
[**If you agree the public deserves the full truth, can you chip in $3 to power Pramila’s fight for transparency and accountability?**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20251226.5r9op0&refcode=em20251226-5745&amount=3&t=2&akid=5745%2E420207%2EGLPrTX)
When a release is stripped of context, explanation, and completeness, it doesn’t build trust — it undermines it. And when the government fails to meet basic expectations, the people have the right to ask, _**“What are they hiding, and who are they protecting?”**_
Jeffrey Epstein abused and exploited young girls, and survivors have spent _almost two decades_ demanding accountability from every powerful person and institution that enabled him.
The least Congress can do is insist on the full truth, and that’s why Pramila voted to release the files, despite the Trump administration and House leadership trying to stall, muddy the waters, and run out the clock. **Because no one — rich, connected, or politically protected — should be above the law.**
[**Pramila will keep pushing for real transparency and real accountability. If you’re with her, can you chip in $3 to help fuel this work?**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20251226.5r9op0&refcode=em20251226-5745&amount=3&t=3&akid=5745%2E420207%2EGLPrTX)
**[Contribute $3](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20251226.5r9op0&amount=3&refcode=em20251226-5745&t=22&akid=5745%2E420207%2EGLPrTX)**
Thank you,
Team Jayapal
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20251226-5745-footer&t=23&refcode2=5745_420207_GLPrTX&akid=5745%2E420207%2EGLPrTX)**

Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.

clean_body: Peter, When we started our process, we never imagined your name would end up on the final unverified list. We frankly all assumed you'd be one of the first people to complete the process and move on. But here we are, hours before the midnight deadline, and your status is still showing as Do you realize what's about to happen, Peter? In a few hours, your Republican Party affiliation will be stamped if not completed before the midnight deadline. ****************************************************************************************************** Did our emails end up in spam? Or have you decided you don't want to be associated with the Republican Party anymore? ****************************************************************************************************** Thank you, NRSC HQ | Paid for by the NRSC. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. |

name: Dexter HQ
subject: Maxine leads with science, not ideology
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maxine for Congress
body:
https://maxinefororegon.com/ [https://maxinefororegon.com/]
Hi Peter,
Donald Trump’s administration demeans physicians, scientists, and public health experts— anyone who speaks the truth.
He filled the U.S. vaccine committee with MAHA cronies, sidelined the experts our nation relies on, and slashed funding for programs like SNAP. Because of Trump’s policies, our families are more vulnerable to economic turbulence and infectious diseases.
Maxine leads with science, not ideology. She shows what’s possible when physicians, scientists, and public health leaders refuse to stay silent.
Here are some of the bills she co-sponsored:
[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_med_email-25q4?refcode=x_251222_longfight_act]
* The Protecting Free Vaccines Act
* The State-Based Universal Health Care Act of 2025
* The Lowering Drug Costs for American Families Act
Dr. Dexter won’t stop advocating for healthcare for families because she believes that everyone deserves access to affordable, comprehensive care, no matter what.
She knows that we don’t have the luxury of waiting for someone else to act. So she’s creating a path for more experts to fight back.
2026 is coming fast, and the fight to take back the House starts now. We need more people like Maxine defending our families in the House to stop Trump’s anti-science agenda in its tracks.
Rush $12 today to keep Maxine in Congress and show the next generation of physicians and scientists that they can run, lead, and make a real difference for our families and our future. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_med_email-25q4?refcode=x_251226_leadswithscience_act&amount=12]
If you have saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately:

Now she's using those skills in Congress to ensure healthy communities by standing up to Big Pharma, fighting climate change, and expanding reproductive rights, as well as rebuilding a fair economy for all by protecting Social Security and Medicare.
To learn more about Maxine, visit MaxineForOregon.com [https://maxinefororegon.com/] .

We need you, Fellow Conservative to keep us on the frontlines, bucking the corrupt status quo! -> If the media wants to attack us for having a "warrior mentality" - the mentality to do whatever it takes to achieve a mission... -> If the media wants to attack us for supporting President Trump's MAGA movement - the movement that says we can make America great... -> If the media wants to attack us for taking on the government corruption, fraud, and waste - which has been exposed by Trump's DOGE department... ...Then they don't understand WHY Donald J. Trump and ALL of us were elected! As SEALs, we all shoot straight with each other. We're going to shoot straight with you, too: This hit piece was horsesht and part of a larger conspiracy to silence us. If they can silence us, they can sure as hell silence you. Thank you so much for your time, Tim Sheehy Eli Crane Ryan Zinke Three Navy SEALs Serving in Congress | " "I SERVED AS A NAVY SEAL, BUILT ONE OF THE LARGEST AERIAL FIREFIGHTING COMPANIES IN AMERICA CREATING OVER 200 MONTANA JOBS, AND STARTED A VETERAN-LED CATTLE RANCHING AND FEEDING OPERATION. WHETHER IT WAS IN WAR OR BUSINESS, I SEE PROBLEMS AND SOLVE THEM. AMERICA NEEDS CONSERVATIVE LEADERS WHO LOVE OUR COUNTRY. I'M RUNNING FOR THE U.S. SENATE BECAUSE OUR CAMPAIGN IS ABOUT SERVICE, DUTY, AND COUNTRY -- NOT POLITICS AS USUAL." * | PAID FOR BY TIM SHEEHY FOR MONTANA Use of Tim Sheehy, Eli Crane, and Ryan Zinke's military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

subject: If Boebert had her way, we’d have only one religion
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
A cornerstone principle for American freedom: the right to worship—or not—however you choose. And Boebert wants it gone.
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001KiRVqNrUWxVxQkuXjBNg7-dyIBGuSO0eTAKBq1ANCTYXc5Qt63D6FFzOhihx736LnV02ZBvL-QT9qGOSdFYPfebypwFndmy40IAfln0RB4GxYKWbOajIf8G-Bnhjkn8vdBNx5eQ8bU-7Pbs9UBPoJalbia0MaqASkf9MrC5moWDPOdbUQ928_ossL1U6nTgCKD96BbvJNMVr2eKxA6h1n3s905Yu1oBJ&c=r4nc4tspliDRUQXldD0qmSX_2QUywt02DAVEuVq0UXvnjtjhE2W3xA==&ch=BVzIMg1LRhItjE_IcQLCcfWFnF-SMMcdNgK_nl_ZJqaGEIIlrd9U9A==)
---
|

Peter,
If Lauren Boebert had her way, the church would be the state. Don't take our word for it--Boebert said this herself:
"The church is supposed to direct the government. The government is not supposed to direct the church. That is not how our Founding Fathers intended it. I'm tired of this separation of church and state junk that's not in the Constitution."
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001KiRVqNrUWxVxQkuXjBNg7-dyIBGuSO0eTAKBq1ANCTYXc5Qt63D6FFzOhihx736LnV02ZBvL-QT9qGOSdFYPfebypwFndmy40IAfln0RB4GxYKWbOajIf8G-Bnhjkn8vdBNx5eQ8bU-7Pbs9UBPoJalbia0MaqASkf9MrC5moWDPOdbUQ928_ossL1U6nTgCKD96BbvJNMU6F2y2h0OfEXpYu70JOXif&c=r4nc4tspliDRUQXldD0qmSX_2QUywt02DAVEuVq0UXvnjtjhE2W3xA==&ch=BVzIMg1LRhItjE_IcQLCcfWFnF-SMMcdNgK_nl_ZJqaGEIIlrd9U9A==)
---
Extremists like Boebert are weaponizing Christian Nationalist ideology to fight for total abortion bans, taxpayer-funded private religious schools, and undoing LGBTQ+ and gender equality--including taking away women's voting rights.
This flies in the face of the Constitution. We have the freedom under the First Amendment to worship however and whatever we choose because America's Founders saw firsthand the danger of state-sanctioned religion.
Boebert and Trump don't believe in freedom. They believe only in submission to MAGA. And they won't stop until they control both the Church and the State.
But we can stop them right now. Trisha Calvarese is our best chance to defeat MAGA extremist Lauren Boebert and put an end to their fascist agenda.
Boebert is vulnerable: she only won re-election because of 546 votes. In 2024, as her Democratic opponent, Trisha had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district, overperforming Kamala Harris by 6 pts as Boebert underperformed Trump in every single county.
[Can you help Trisha defeat Lauren Boebert with a donation of $5, $25, $50 or whatever you can do right now? Our end of quarter deadline is fast approaching and MAGA will be watching to see if we have what it takes to win this election. Let's show them:](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001KiRVqNrUWxVxQkuXjBNg7-dyIBGuSO0eTAKBq1ANCTYXc5Qt63D6FFzOhihx736LVtJJIIacA1OknIfij_HZ3vxeHy9ERhySXzLtOi-gWNNIUSeDsFPWOCsO-NgEB4d-qzi_XT76PiZDV72M0J-RYWMFlyJ3FKP_ZZX7e2QpuuTdD0QQiX2CNmw8e9RIIkG5kz_w2WCEKyvhHNv9-Bbie-pXRUx5K_E6EtAkO002gqM=&c=r4nc4tspliDRUQXldD0qmSX_2QUywt02DAVEuVq0UXvnjtjhE2W3xA==&ch=BVzIMg1LRhItjE_IcQLCcfWFnF-SMMcdNgK_nl_ZJqaGEIIlrd9U9A==)

clean_body: Laura Gillen is the number one target in the country for Republicans. | = | Peter, A new Politico report just made something very clear: Laura Gillen is the number one target in the country for Republicans. Experts say Republicans believe they can win this district and will spend an unlimited amount of money to defeat her. Laura won by only 2 points last cycle. This is the only toss-up district in New York and one of the seats that will determine whether Democrats take back the House in 2026. If Republicans flip it, they keep their majority and can continue pushing Trump’s agenda with no check. = | | | = | = | = | = | = Thank you for standing with us, Team Gillen * | | = Paid for and authorized by Gillen for NY. name: Wes Moore
subject: I wish this request could wait until next year.
body:
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=1119%2E1086043%2Exa7EPc)
Hey, Peter. It’s Wes.
I’m writing to you today to first and foremost say _thank you_ for the support you’ve given me this year. I’m filled with gratitude for the trust, encouragement, and hope the people of Maryland have placed in me as we fight together to build a state that leaves no one behind.
**[I’m also emailing to ask, if it makes sense for your budget, if you’d make one $5 contribution to my campaign for reelection ahead of our final deadline of the year.](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251226-1.mMvAUO&refcode=em-20251226-1&t=2&akid=1119%2E1086043%2Exa7EPc)**
I realize this request comes at a time when most folks are spending time with loved ones around the holidays. Or maybe you’re just trying to unplug until the new year. Please know that I don’t make this ask lightly.
**The reason this request simply can’t wait until the new year is because on January 14, my campaign will legally have to pause all fundraising during the Maryland State Legislative session.** That means 11:59 on 12/31 is our last quarterly deadline before this fundraising freeze. It’s crucial we don’t fall behind in this moment, before our fundraising will be legally paused for a period of around three full months.
Trust me when I say that I’d have waited until the new year to ask for your support if I could. And I know that at this point in the year post-holidays, many folks may not choose to contribute. **[But if you’re willing to be among the few people in Cresco who can donate even one time before our final deadline of 2025, I’d be so grateful.](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251226-1.mMvAUO&refcode=em-20251226-1&t=3&akid=1119%2E1086043%2Exa7EPc)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251226-1.mMvAUO&amount=5&refcode=em-20251226-1&t=22&akid=1119%2E1086043%2Exa7EPc)**
Thank you for hearing me out during this quieter holiday week. Dawn, the kids, and I are wishing you a peaceful and safe New Year.
Elevate,
Wes
[](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251226-1.mMvAUO&refcode=em-20251226-1&t=23&akid=1119%2E1086043%2Exa7EPc)

* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-footer?refcode=em-20251226-1&t=24&refcode2=1119_1086043_xa7EPc&akid=1119%2E1086043%2Exa7EPc)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=25&akid=1119%2E1086043%2Exa7EPc)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.wesmoore.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=26&akid=1119%2E1086043%2Exa7EPc)

Email us: [info@wesmoore.com](mailto:info@wesmoore.com)
BY AUTHORITY: WES MOORE FOR MARYLAND, MARY TYDINGS, TREASURER

name: Team Tran
subject: Here’s where we stand:
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122018524/591318021/-1516755731?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDg3ODciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZDMyNTBiYmItODRlMi1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=PbNUWIdXltKdoA2XJjBCF3C5LFyGsmdLgIMRHcnoUg0=)
Friend -- there are just a few days left in 2025, so here’s where things stand:

**Here’s the good news:** We’re building real momentum. Thousands of grassroots supporters have already chipped in, our field team is ramping up for 2026, and we’re closing out the year stronger than we started.

**But here’s the hard truth:** We’re still short of our final public fundraising goal of the year. And with the FEC deadline on the 31st, what we report will be dissected by Republican operatives looking for signs of weakness.

We can’t afford to miss this goal. Derek flipped CA-45 by just 653 votes -- and GOP insiders are already circling, with rumors that a major Republican figure could enter the race to try and take this seat back.

body:
Our hands are tied

| It’s been less than a year under the Trump administration and the consequences of their reckless policies are already clear:

_MAGA Republicans passed the largest cut to Medicaid in history.

For the first time in decades, Social Security is at risk for complete dissolution.

MAGA Republicans broke the record for the longest government shutdown.

Americans are struggling to pay their rent, utilities, and groceries._

**[We can’t afford to wait and hope things get better. name: Adam Schiff
subject: The gravity of the situation
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122019447/591321198/148511826?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE1MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYjdlYzE0Yi04NWUyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=s9AGCG_HV3HOUQzqOJ4XqyTnwfcRTMOalQHzoO7scVs=)
Hi Peter, this is Adam Schiff.

Lately, I’ve been thinking a lot about the gravity of the situation we’re facing.

**[I want to share some thoughts with you — but before I do, will you pitch in any amount to my campaign and George Whitesides before our end-of-quarter deadline?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122019450/591321201/-1342768506?refcode=em-12.26.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE1MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYjdlYzE0Yi04NWUyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=s9AGCG_HV3HOUQzqOJ4XqyTnwfcRTMOalQHzoO7scVs=)**
| ****[PITCH IN](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122019452/591321205/-1342768506?refcode=em-12.26.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE1MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYjdlYzE0Yi04NWUyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=s9AGCG_HV3HOUQzqOJ4XqyTnwfcRTMOalQHzoO7scVs=)****
---

We are living in unprecedented times. Never before has our country faced such deep threats to our democracy.

Health insurance costs are ballooning because they took money out of health care for a tax cut for rich people. People are worried about making ends meet. Housing costs are skyrocketing.

Meanwhile, Trump and his allies in Congress are more interested in going after their rivals than fixing these economic crises.

**It's a lot, and I understand why you might want a break.**

But we can’t afford to disengage or turn away. There are too many important fights that we must continue to wage — for an economy that works for everyone, for our democracy, and for the health and safety of us all.

**The future is not set; it is up to us, and we can make all the difference.**

And there are good reasons for hope. Millions of Americans have come out to protest against the president. Courts are ruling in our favor. And we scored important victories last month in California, New Jersey, and Virginia.

**[To prepare for the battles ahead, I'm humbly asking for your support today. So Peter, can I count on you to chip in any amount you can to George Whitesides and me to support my fight to defend our democracy and deliver for working people? clean_body: | = Hi Peter, this is Adam Schiff. Lately, I’ve been thinking a lot about the gravity of the situation we’re facing. = | **= We are living in unprecedented times. Never before has our country faced such deep threats to our democracy. Health insurance costs are ballooning because they took money out of health care for a tax cut for rich people. People are worried about making ends meet. Housing costs are skyrocketing. Meanwhile, Trump and his allies in Congress are more interested in going after their rivals than fixing these economic crises. It's a lot, and I understand why you might want a break. But we can’t afford to disengage or turn away. There are too many important fights that we must continue to wage — for an economy that works for everyone, for our democracy, and for the health and safety of us all. The future is not set; it is up to us, and we can make all the difference. And there are good reasons for hope. Millions of Americans have come out to protest against the president. Courts are ruling in our favor. And we scored important victories last month in California, New Jersey, and Virginia. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | =** Thank you for being part of this grassroots team and having my back every step of the way. — Adam | = = = George has spent his career solving problems. name: Squad Victory Fund
subject: The clock is ticking — and the Squad needs you
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20251226-1766-foote&t=1001&akid=1766%2E342411%2ED78V7K ]Squad Victory Fund

Peter,

2025 is almost over, and thanks to supporters like you, the Squad has made
incredible progress this year. We’ve organized in communities, pushed bold
progressive initiatives, and built the foundation for real change.

But how we finish this year will set the tone for everything we do in 2026
— and that’s why we need your help right now.

We’ve set an ambitious end-of-year goal of $150,000, and we have until
Wednesday at midnight to hit it. Every contribution strengthens our
organizing, fuels our people-powered movement, and gives champions like
Alexandria, Ayanna, Ilhan, and Rashida the tools they need to keep
fighting for working families, healthcare, economic justice, and so much
more.

Hitting this goal is a statement that shows the strength and determination
of our movement. It proves that when the Squad and our supporters come
together, we can accomplish anything. And it sets the stage for 2026,
giving us the momentum and resources we need to start the year strong and
unstoppable.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20251226-movement.Vww8Ui&refcode=em20251226-1766&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=1766%2E342411%2ED78V7K ]That’s why we’re asking you to chip in $3 before
Wednesday’s deadline — your support right now can make all the difference
in how we start next year.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20251226-movement.Vww8Ui&refcode=em20251226-1766&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=1766%2E342411%2ED78V7K ]Contribute ››

Let’s finish this year with everything we’ve got. Let’s show the world the
strength of our movement. And let’s start 2026 stronger than ever.

In solidarity,

The Squad Victory Fund

Paid for by The Squad Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated equally among: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

clean_body: It started with a tweet... and now it’s war. Elon Musk just humiliated Donald Trump in front of millions — live on X . | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | It started with a tweet... and now it’s war. name: Pennsylvania Working Families Party
subject: Your PA WFP Newsletter: Wrapping up 2025, building toward 2026
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pennsylvania Working Families Party
body:
Thank you for an incredible year — here’s what we’re carrying into 2026. | | | Hi Peter, As we close out 2025, we wanted to take a moment to say thank you, and to share what we’ve been building together over the past month. **From celebrating in community to setting our sights on the year ahead, here’s where things landed — and where we’re going next:**
* * *
**_🎉 Closing Out 2025 Together_** Over the past few weeks, Working Families across Pennsylvania came together to reflect, reconnect, and look ahead. At our **PA WFP Holiday Fundraiser** , we gathered with **Councilmembers Kendra Brooks** and **Nic O’Rourke** , supporters, and organizers from across the movement — and thanks to you, we raised over _$20,000_ to fuel our work heading into 2026. The room was full of warmth, energy, and a shared commitment to building real power for working people. [](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqt1KgGElNLHWwWbO_pXXNSC3nN4d_2mAbw4hNJ60jOiJr5xN6Yk1reyfmO0B-j8w8-0ysWZvBtx2m5bbRddf1TKwYIp2oEAjJnoN7UkCQ0C1uw_5ot_cWon7fDPq4xt-tTe_cANMUBagRWeWfuoOgxEZDmmN-NDno5VSU3tOyghBhVY59Le04Tg2D8bWM0ll3-0L-A-o5KGRVtCj3gPqKDQEbVIvdbEvDWICcIh_hzOho1L8pgYUyeibEpdWZlNO93l2ESYEb2TQOzfAcGwtr8O7ajARpi7NJuFW6oEGQymt9Ts_85u75HjFwIr5IAJmA5SfEdO3AOhSS0cl16NVpX1hHmoieGPf-7C6iVc-y-q-hbLLhKp1CcS2_RHjtdPSHjKKXg6_VEWWoZo1Yw-LOubuOnjzCbgcrklCsfo6bWwRcpTm05e5B2VGdGjgnqC5lk/4mr/-oA3LgvcSPCrtbTgv6lT_A/h0/h001.lS5Eez_wmNMlvgnYFw7ctJ1s-gkDGpxFsdhTbR8ycAM) We also wrapped up the year with our **Statewide Virtual Member Gathering** , bringing together members from across the Commonwealth to share regional updates, lift up election wins, and talk honestly about what this moment demands of us. Taken together, these spaces were a reminder of what makes this movement special: people showing up for one another, across counties and communities, with clarity and care. Thank you for being part of it — whether you joined live or supported from afar.
* * *
**_✊ Carrying This Momentum Into 2026_** As we head into the new year, a few things are clear:
* Organizing works when it’s rooted in community
* Training and political education matter
* Regular people deserve real support to run for office
* Solidarity isn’t seasonal — it’s a practice
* We can and will beat the billionaires

Those lessons are shaping everything we’re building next. We’re entering 2026 with focus, momentum, and big plans to keep growing working-class power across Pennsylvania.
* * *
**_RSVP — January 13th: Working Families Power Immigrant Solidarity (ICE) Training_** **** [**One way these values are being put into action is through an Immigrant Solidarity Training on Tuesday, January 13th, from 6:00 - 8:00 PM in West Philadelphia.**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMbGj-ca0TYSsYMZBvIweYdKKRAVkNJZnW5zZGMfWrPRUF_Wz0xUTm_L4V0dpuC1mJbS0qb03G-4p0qH7domKQA0LkQHC67soGY15lthpHm3aXJOS-j9iB8-cSnTEqbhByLcP9s1Clx16CvetJPfR7lpCFFmYxmxs_74L1dr4GpDKCKscMKiUO46hyVvlk4DcJkdYpJbds3kcGP2n5U6jtUv0mPEK6KtLvfvMeIhp9hRUPlHUYkoSkhoiAQip8DwoaT_awe0a2u1SysimTEdnaD63bVr6wkdgxPUCigI7rfHk/4mr/-oA3LgvcSPCrtbTgv6lT_A/h1/h001.vgPv6wBemXK7DJ3B5aueBgg919nGLLd8k3ZxYCELz4o) This training will help participants gain the knowledge, skills, and confidence to help uphold their rights — and the rights of their neighbors — during an ICE raid in Philadelphia. The training will be led by **Working Families Power** , with participation from the staff of the Offices of **Councilmember Kendra Brooks, Nicolas O’Rourke, Rue Landau, and Jamie Gauthier** , **State Rep. Rick Krajewski** , and **Philly DSA**. 📅 Tuesday, January 13th, 2026
⏰ 6:00 - 8:00 PM
📍 Location shared after RSVP (West Philadelphia) **[RSVP here](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMbGj-ca0TYSsYMZBvIweYdKKRAVkNJZnW5zZGMfWrPRUF_Wz0xUTm_L4V0dpuC1mJbS0qb03G-4p0qH7domKQA2yOd9jTqWFAWGkYgtGs5X50mZwGLCADBpVzKnIDNkB-w42DPmCK9GtgDsz7DKfCUAsgkejIyFyv4_sPIsOLGAnoFPnMH8yjC7DYzQAIciyrnqcYdCT4HW9xywgu3vyhAi9-Pf6o_MQFV64OW-WWLiWgi-Q5WhD6D5T0ygExfmpkp2ru1IegMb7QqmOfL7qbdyt9iO0xdEKy2u-wgtLaw2Y/4mr/-oA3LgvcSPCrtbTgv6lT_A/h2/h001.THoIzln9ApcyASvYHzc7xua8CT39_g4u8zH8Pmmfpkk)**
* * *
**_💰 In 2026, We are Taxing Billionaires to Fund PA!_ ** Across Pennsylvania, families are struggling with bills for groceries, heat, rent, and childcare. The cost of a decent life keeps rising while our paychecks barely budge. Public services like healthcare, schools, and transit are consistently on the chopping block. These cuts would make basic necessities even more expensive. Meanwhile, a handful of billionaires are hoarding wealth, cozying up to politicians, hijacking politics for their own profit, while dodging taxes they could easily pay. We refuse to accept a system where a tiny elite calls all the shots while the rest of us struggle. **** [**On January 13th, we're launching Tax Billionaires, Fund PA — a statewide campaign to raise billions of dollars for our schools, healthcare, public transit, and housing by asking the people with the most to pay just a little bit more.**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.v2UrIOJHHKkg0Pk-aGvhy2ldtYd_E7WKWxeQ_Z2CVmj8ZfKhw3Q1O4fGy5pG22MD4ebmhGV6KpjuoT4E_AelEtStCptbkCZzw3lnt1HwJ5-MaO9GYaEsuqmLsjJnlvJal1OU8ypkUGPhScVIrm_pDHo8dniYac7S-2z6hjpa5VrAz5WwrI70bQFcI2ZSy6s4D5crl6kdH8Butpp_BXDRB7ZvNKcJgoW5ouMY_sdPz4zqYWOD3oQqNX_0ex0IMxp1WWchrpdm-0zt40xygJaRUseKp02acpLj-Vljcr114UFHMaks910F1PgZRyZThzumV5nye1r95-ZbJGKVQw9A1ibsM9igCgKX_8fotyja121ZBQyxZ13ZVL0JS-r9tkzPxIp_CfKiZuQOyradjOMPcvkXbvNIB0eKdw8-DVfC_mo/4mr/-oA3LgvcSPCrtbTgv6lT_A/h3/h001.3o7UF7S9-D06PdNrUbPjvpgn5WlgZjQXBSitnshFjUk) 📅 Tuesday, January 13th, 2026

clean_body: Thank you for an incredible year — here’s what we’re carrying into 2026. | | | Hi Peter, As we close out 2025, we wanted to take a moment to say thank you, and to share what we’ve been building together over the past month. From celebrating in community to setting our sights on the year ahead, here’s where things landed — and where we’re going next: 🎉 Closing Out 2025 Together Over the past few weeks, Working Families across Pennsylvania came together to reflect, reconnect, and look ahead. At our PA WFP Holiday Fundraiser , we gathered with Councilmembers Kendra Brooks and Nic O’Rourke , supporters, and organizers from across the movement — and thanks to you, we raised over $20,000 to fuel our work heading into 2026. The room was full of warmth, energy, and a shared commitment to building real power for working people. We also wrapped up the year with our Statewide Virtual Member Gathering , bringing together members from across the Commonwealth to share regional updates, lift up election wins, and talk honestly about what this moment demands of us. Taken together, these spaces were a reminder of what makes this movement special: people showing up for one another, across counties and communities, with clarity and care. Thank you for being part of it — whether you joined live or supported from afar. ✊ Carrying This Momentum Into 2026 As we head into the new year, a few things are clear: Organizing works when it’s rooted in community Training and political education matter Regular people deserve real support to run for office Solidarity isn’t seasonal — it’s a practice We can and will beat the billionaires Those lessons are shaping everything we’re building next. We’re entering 2026 with focus, momentum, and big plans to keep growing working-class power across Pennsylvania. Meanwhile, a handful of billionaires are hoarding wealth, cozying up to politicians, hijacking politics for their own profit, while dodging taxes they could easily pay. We refuse to accept a system where a tiny elite calls all the shots while the rest of us struggle. ** 📅 Tuesday, January 13th, 2026 ⏰ 6:00 - 7:00 PM 📍Virtual 🗳️ Thinking About Running for Office? If 2025 taught us anything, it’s that regular people stepping up can change what’s possible. If you’ve ever thought, “Someone like me should be in office,” we agree. name: Colin Allred
subject: I’m running for Congress
body:
Peter,
I officially ended my campaign for U.S. Senate and filed to run for Congress in Texas’ 33rd Congressional District.
I didn’t come to this decision lightly – I’ve put everything I had into my Senate campaign over the last five months, traveling to every corner of the state, talking to as many Texans as I could, building on the incredible grassroots campaign we started last year.
While it was clear that many Texans were ready to support me to the end, it was also clear that the Democratic primary would end up in a bruising and costly runoff in May. And if we want to prevent another MAGA loyalist from representing our state for six more years, that’s simply a risk we can’t afford to take.
So, today I am announcing my campaign to represent the newly drawn 33rd district in Texas. If you’d still like to see me head to Washington in November, I hope you’ll consider splitting a donation between me and Real Texas to get my new campaign off to a strong start today. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-allred?refcode=122625_2]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Colin Allred and The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately:
DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-allred?express_lane=true&refcode=122625_2&amount=5]
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-allred?express_lane=true&refcode=122625_2&amount=10]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-allred?express_lane=true&refcode=122625_2&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-allred?express_lane=true&refcode=122625_2&amount=100]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-allred?express_lane=true&refcode=122625_2&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-allred?refcode=122625_2&amount=25]
District 33 was racially gerrymandered by Republicans in an effort to further rig our democracy, but it’s still the community where I grew up attending public schools and watching my mom struggle to pay for our groceries. It’s the community where Aly and I are raising our two boys. It is my home.
The threats that our democracy and our way of living are facing right now are real. It’s harder than ever for kids like me to get a shot, and for single moms like mine to afford their basic needs. But I am more confident than I have ever been that together, we can beat these threats. That’s why I am humbly asking for your help to return to Congress to fight for North Texans, our democracy, and the American Dream.
If you can, please split a donation of a few bucks between my campaign and Real Texas to help me get started. Thank you for reading, and for all of your continued support as we fight to get Texas representation we can be proud of. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-allred?refcode=122625_2]
Colin Allred

Contributions or gifts to The Real Texas PAC are not tax deductible. Contributions from corporations, labor organizations, treasury funds, and federal government contractors are prohibited. Only U.S. citizens or legal permanent residents are eligible to contribute. An Individual can contribute up to $5,000 to The Real Texas PAC per calendar year. Married couples may together give a total of $10,000 per calendar year. Federal political action committees (PACs) can contribute up to $5,000 per calendar year. The first $5,000 contributed in a calendar year from federally permissible sources will be deposited in The Real Texas PAC's contribution account. Once a federally permissible source has contributed $5,000 in a calendar year, The Real Texas PAC will transfer any amounts in excess of $5,000 to its non-contribution account. All contributions from corporations and labor unions will be deposited in the non-contribution account. Please contact us at realtexas@mbacg.com if you would like a different allocation.

clean_body: Peter, I officially ended my campaign for U.S. Senate and filed to run for Congress in Texas’ 33rd Congressional District. I didn’t come to this decision lightly – I’ve put everything I had into my Senate campaign over the last five months, traveling to every corner of the state, talking to as many Texans as I could, building on the incredible grassroots campaign we started last year. While it was clear that many Texans were ready to support me to the end, it was also clear that the Democratic primary would end up in a bruising and costly runoff in May. And if we want to prevent another MAGA loyalist from representing our state for six more years, that’s simply a risk we can’t afford to take. So, today I am announcing my campaign to represent the newly drawn 33rd district in Texas. If you’d still like to see me head to Washington in November, I hope you’ll consider splitting a donation between me and Real Texas to get my new campaign off to a strong start today. [] If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Colin Allred and The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately: DONATE $5 [] DONATE $10 [] DONATE $50 [] DONATE $100 [] DONATE $250 [] Other Amount [] District 33 was racially gerrymandered by Republicans in an effort to further rig our democracy, but it’s still the community where I grew up attending public schools and watching my mom struggle to pay for our groceries. It’s the community where Aly and I are raising our two boys. It is my home. The threats that our democracy and our way of living are facing right now are real. It’s harder than ever for kids like me to get a shot, and for single moms like mine to afford their basic needs. But I am more confident than I have ever been that together, we can beat these threats. That’s why I am humbly asking for your help to return to Congress to fight for North Texans, our democracy, and the American Dream. If you can, please split a donation of a few bucks between my campaign and Real Texas to help me get started. Thank you for reading, and for all of your continued support as we fight to get Texas representation we can be proud of. [] Colin Allred [] [] The Real Texas PAC 1150 N Loop 1604 W Ste 108-230 San Antonio, TX 78248 United States Contributions or gifts to The Real Texas PAC are not tax deductible. Contributions from corporations, labor organizations, treasury funds, and federal government contractors are prohibited. Only U.S. citizens or legal permanent residents are eligible to contribute. An Individual can contribute up to $5,000 to The Real Texas PAC per calendar year. Married couples may together give a total of $10,000 per calendar year. Federal political action committees (PACs) can contribute up to $5,000 per calendar year. The first $5,000 contributed in a calendar year from federally permissible sources will be deposited in The Real Texas PAC's contribution account. Once a federally permissible source has contributed $5,000 in a calendar year, The Real Texas PAC will transfer any amounts in excess of $5,000 to its non-contribution account. All contributions from corporations and labor unions will be deposited in the non-contribution account. Please contact us at realtexas@mbacg.com if you would like a different allocation. If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

name: Gabby Giffords
subject: We cannot let this become reality.
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
#

| | | | | | | | | | |
---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---
Hi there, it's Gabby Giffords. Today, I’m reaching out to respectfully ask you to make your first contribution of $3 to GIFFORDS PAC.

**This is urgent: House Republicans are preparing to pass a bill for a nationwide concealed carry mandate.**

If passed, it would force states to allow people to carry hidden, loaded guns anywhere—even if they don’t meet the safety standards of that state or locality.

That means someone from a state with no rules about who can buy or carry weapons could come into your town, neighborhood, or business—and there’s nothing we could do to stop them.

This is unacceptable. GIFFORDS is working to build the largest mobilization program since Donald Trump took office. All of this takes a lot of resources, which is why I’m asking:

[**Will you please chip in before midnight to help GIFFORDS PAC defeat the GOP’s concealed carry mandate?**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-q42025.UDsFRy&refcode=em251226-1-b&t=1&akid=11127%2E3935704%2E5cvUTG) Gabby Giffords |
**[DONATE](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-q42025.UDsFRy&refcode=em251226-1-b&t=2&akid=11127%2E3935704%2E5cvUTG)**

Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.Giffords.org
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. We try to send only the most important information and opportunities to participate via email, but click [here](https://go.giffords.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=3&akid=11127%2E3935704%2E5cvUTG) if you'd like to unsubscribe. Giffords PAC was started to support elected officials who step up to fight the gun violence epidemic. Thank you for standing with Gabby and the Giffords team.

name: Berrett Gall
subject: 5 more days
disclaimer_text: paid for by Walz for Governor
body:
Here’s why your contribution is so urgent.
# [](https://act.timwalz.com/go/195?refcode=em20251226-352&t=1&akid=352%2E34582%2EoFA9nv)
Hey Peter, I hope you’re having a happy and restful holiday with your loved ones.
I’m reaching out to ask if you can **[pitch in before our ONLY public filing deadline of the year](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251226_1.BK2irC&amount=5&refcode=em20251226-352&t=2&akid=352%2E34582%2EoFA9nv)** , and provide an explanation as to why your contribution is so urgent.
**We only have 5 more days until we close the books on 2025.** Everything we’ve raised up to that point will be shared for everyone — including over a dozen GOP opponents and all the political pundits — to see.
Those numbers don’t just sit on a spreadsheet. They’ll shape the media narrative, influence who else jumps into this race, and signal whether we have the momentum to win in 2026.
We must send a clear message that the people stand with Tim and refuse to let Trump-backed Mike Lindell or any other right-wing extremist take Minnesota backward.
Once we ring in the New Year, we only have ten months to reach voters and defeat the MAGA movement on Election Day. That’s really not a lot of time, and we need the financial resources as soon as possible to make sure we can run a winning campaign.
**[So, can Tim count on you to pitch in $5 toward our $100,000 goal before next Wednesday’s end-of-year deadline?](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251226_1.BK2irC&amount=5&refcode=em20251226-352&t=3&akid=352%2E34582%2EoFA9nv)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251226_1.BK2irC&amount=5&refcode=20251226-352&t=22&akid=352%2E34582%2EoFA9nv)**
Let’s make Tim proud and keep Minnesota on a path to progress.
Berrett Gall
Senior Advisor
Walz for Governor

**[Donate ››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_tw_footer?refcode=em20251226-352&t=23&refcode2=352_34582_oFA9nv&akid=352%2E34582%2EoFA9nv)**

name: AOC
subject: We can make a new world.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
#
I grew up in a working-class family.
My mom cleaned houses and my dad worked to build a small business. My parents worked themselves to the bone so that my brother and I could just have a shot.
And for a moment there, it looked like we were going to make it: we had a home, bills were a little less stressful to pay, and I was doing well in school. What may have looked like a modest life to others was a dream come true for us.
But suddenly, when I was a teenager, my dad was diagnosed with a rare form of cancer. He passed away in September of 2008, just as the economy crashed in the Great Recession and millions of jobs across this country vanished.
It felt like the world of our family fell apart as the larger world was falling apart too.
Overnight, my mom was left to figure out how to provide for two kids by herself, put them through school, keep our house, pay off my dad's medical bills — all on a house cleaner salary.
That's how I became a waitress. Because it was millions of innocent working families who paid the price, while the billionaires and thieves who defrauded our financial system got off richer than ever before. **And not a single one of them went to jail**.
And you know what?
**_That_ is the story that Republicans mock — not just mine, but all of ours — when they say a waitress or working person is unqualified to serve in Congress**.
I tell this story not because it is special, but because of how _common_ it is.
While the details may differ, so many of us know what it feels like for life to be one bad day, one bad piece of news, one major setback from everything feeling like it's going to fall apart.
**We don't have to live like this anymore**.
We can make a new world, a better country, where we can fight for the dignity of all people.
And it looks like **living wages**.
It looks like **stable housing**.
It looks like **guaranteed health care**.
And it looks like **respect for all of our differences** , no matter who we are or where we come from.
That means that we must choose to out-organize the oligarchy. We will and must do away with the power of big money.
And that is why, even as a waitress with a long shot campaign, I decided that **I would never take money from lobbyists or corporations — and I never will**.
[Will you make a $5 contribution right now to help us fight for progress?](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20251226.oreMJp&refcode=em20251226-10471-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=10471%2E2526796%2EBecjvh)
Every donation helps us mobilize, organize, and fight for the working class in the places that need it most.
In solidarity,
Alexandria

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20251226?refcode=em20251226-10471-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=2&refcode2=10471_2526796_Becjvh&akid=10471%2E2526796%2EBecjvh)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20251226-10471-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=3&akid=10471%2E2526796%2EBecjvh)**
---|---
[](https://www.facebook.com/OcasioCortez/?t=4&akid=10471%2E2526796%2EBecjvh) | [](https://twitter.com/AOC?t=5&akid=10471%2E2526796%2EBecjvh) | [](https://www.instagram.com/aoc/?t=6&akid=10471%2E2526796%2EBecjvh) | [](https://bsky.app/profile/aoc.bsky.social?t=7&akid=10471%2E2526796%2EBecjvh)
---|---|---|---
Email us: [speak@ocasiocortez.com](mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.

name: Laura Friedman
subject: My friend, Hilda Solis
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Laura Friedman for Congress
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122020641/591325040/-1798313451?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM1MzYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNDlhZTExZWQtODllMi1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=xX36pg2HamukTYsi_mDg-P7vmydpXH4-3hw22aGYUN8=) Friend, I'm excited to say that we have a chance to bring another woman with experience and a proven record of success back to Congress: **My friend, Hilda Solis.** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122020642/591325052/-1004990808?refcode=em-12.26.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM1MzYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNDlhZTExZWQtODllMi1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=xX36pg2HamukTYsi_mDg-P7vmydpXH4-3hw22aGYUN8=) Hilda is a trailblazer. Not only was she the first Latina ever elected to the State Senate, but was the first Latina to serve as Secretary of Labor under President Obama. And over the past several years, I have worked with Hilda to provide better educational outcomes for families in Los Angeles. I’m convinced that she’s the right person to continue that work in Congress. Hilda has been a force for good in LA County – by helping expand healthcare access, combating homelessness, advocating for environmental justice, and much more. But the new districts created by Proposition 50 means she’s starting from ground zero and needs our immediate support to finish Q4 in a strong position. [**Hilda Solis is the candidate in CA-38 we need to start the blue wave in Congress. clean_body: | = Friend, I'm excited to say that we have a chance to bring another woman with experience and a proven record of success back to Congress: My friend, Hilda Solis. = Hilda is a trailblazer. Not only was she the first Latina ever elected to the State Senate, but was the first Latina to serve as Secretary of Labor under President Obama. And over the past several years, I have worked with Hilda to provide better educational outcomes for families in Los Angeles. I’m convinced that she’s the right person to continue that work in Congress. Hilda has been a force for good in LA County – by helping expand healthcare access, combating homelessness, advocating for environmental justice, and much more. But the new districts created by Proposition 50 means she’s starting from ground zero and needs our immediate support to finish Q4 in a strong position. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation will go through immediately: | **= | = ---|--- = | = = | =** Thank you for your support, Laura | | Paid for by Laura Friedman for Congress Laura's campaign doesn't take a dime from corporate PACs and completely relies on grassroots supporters like you! After December 31st, we’ll close the books on 2025. On January 10, our fundraising numbers will be public for the world to see. The Federal Election Commission (FEC) requires every campaign to publicly report how much we’ve raised and how much we’ve spent. This transparency is so important to me. Voters in Coachella Valley know that I answer directly to them and only to them, and our FEC report is part of how we prove it. I’m proud that my campaign has always been powered by grassroots supporters. This report is also how we prove that those same grassroots supporters are standing strong with our campaign against false attacks from the Republican strategists in DC. A strong grassroots showing on our FEC report will prove that the blue wave we’ve seen in 2025 is carrying through to 2026. If we’re going to flip the House, take back control, and reverse the red-leaning trend we’ve seen here in CA-25, we need to push hard, right now. The son of hardworking farmworkers, Dr. Ruiz grew up in Coachella, CA and learned at an early age that the key to attaining the American Dream is hard work and a great education. In the summer of 1990, Raul walked in the desert heat from business to business in the Coachella Valley asking them to invest in their community by contributing to his education. With each investment for college, he made a promise to come back home and serve his community as a physician. In 2012, he decided to take his service to the community to the next level by running for Congress. **Dr. Ruiz needs our help to continue fighting for us in Congress.**

clean_body: The final FEC deadline of 2025 is this Wednesday, December 31, 2025. While Washington remains gridlocked, Dr. Raul Ruiz is committed to serving and helping constituents with the problems they have with the federal government and cutting red tape. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | The final FEC deadline of 2025 is this Wednesday, December 31, 2025. = = I want to take a moment to explain what is about to happen. After December 31st, we’ll close the books on 2025. On January 10, our fundraising numbers will be public for the world to see. The Federal Election Commission (FEC) requires every campaign to publicly report how much we’ve raised and how much we’ve spent. This transparency is so important to me. Voters in Coachella Valley know that I answer directly to them and only to them, and our FEC report is part of how we prove it. I’m proud that my campaign has always been powered by grassroots supporters. This report is also how we prove that those same grassroots supporters are standing strong with our campaign against false attacks from the Republican strategists in DC. A strong grassroots showing on our FEC report will prove that the blue wave we’ve seen in 2025 is carrying through to 2026. If we’re going to flip the House, take back control, and reverse the red-leaning trend we’ve seen here in CA-25, we need to push hard, right now. = If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately. | | = | = | = | = | = | = I truly appreciate your commitment to stopping CA-25 from going red in 2026. name: Team Mandela Barnes
subject: Here are our 5 reasons
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mandela for Wisconsin
body:
We’re at a critical moment, and this is our pitch to you
| Friend, there’s just 5 days left in the year – that means time is running out to reach our end-of-year fundraising goal. **[If any of these 5 reasons to donate matters to you, will you chip in to help Mandela Barnes hit the ground running in January?](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=mb-em-fr-dec25.SSbw4W&amount=15&refcode=MB_EM_FR_122625_B1_5Reasons&t=2&akid=5742%2E196590%2EH3bh4C)** | | [CHIP IN $5 »](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=mb-em-fr-dec25.SSbw4W&amount=15&refcode=MB_EM_FR_122625_B1_5Reasons&t=3&akid=5742%2E196590%2EH3bh4C)
---
**1\. Wisconsin has always been a crucial battleground, and next year will be no different.** Your early investment in the race could help Mandela tip the balance and prevent a MAGA extremist from leading Wisconsin.
**2\. Governors serve as the last line of defense against Trump’s agenda.** This race could determine the future for Wisconsin’s families, farmers, and workers.
**3\. Working families are already struggling, and Mandela is ready to step up to the plate.** It will take transformational and innovative leadership to meet this critical moment for Wisconsin.
**4\. This election is a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to build a Democratic trifecta in the state of Wisconsin.** And it would allow us to actually defend our state and legislate for a better future.
**5\. Mandela’s already leading in the polls, but dark money is going to flood this race.** We’re relying on grassroots donors who share Mandela’s vision to help him widen the gap.
